هيفاء الجار الله</orig>
document.document_summary: This issue of the Kuwait Today Official Gazette, dated April 27, 2025, provides a comprehensive overview of various governmental and commercial activities in Kuwait. It includes decrees from the Council of Ministers, such as appointments and nationality withdrawals, and administrative decisions from ministries like Justice, Information, Interior, and Commerce and Industry, covering topics from bankruptcy to media licenses and traffic fines. A significant portion is dedicated to public tenders and announcements from numerous entities including the Central Agency for Public Tenders, Kuwait Oil Company, Ministry of Health, and Kuwait University, detailing requirements, deadlines, and awards for a wide range of projects and services. The gazette also features official announcements regarding company liquidations, commercial agency registrations, general assembly meeting minutes for various companies, public auctions for vehicles and real estate, and a series of criminal judgment announcements from Kuwaiti courts.

chunks.title: Appointing a Member from the Industrial Sector to the Board of Directors of the Public Authority for Industry <orig>الهيئة العامة للصناعة</orig>
chunks.text_en: - After reviewing the Constitution, - And on the Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And on Law No. 56 of 1996 regarding the issuance of the Industry Law, and its amending laws, - And on Decree No. 84 of 2024 regarding Ministerial Succession and Delegations, and its amending decrees, - And based on the submission of the Minister of Commerce and Industry , - And after the approval of the Council of Ministers , We have decreed the following:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Mr. / Faisal Abdulaziz Faisal Al-Zain is appointed as a member of the Board of Directors of the Public Authority for Industry - representing the industrial sector - for a renewable term of three years.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The Minister of Commerce and Industry shall implement this Decree, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette . Amir of Kuwait Mishal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yousef Saud Al-Sabah Minister of Commerce and Industry Khalifa Abdullah Dhahi Al-Ajeel Al-Askar Issued at Seif Palace on: 24 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 22 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Regarding the Withdrawal of Kuwaiti Citizenship Certificates From Certain Individuals
chunks.text_en: The Council of Ministers - Having reviewed the Constitution, - And Amiri Order issued on 2 Dhu al-Qi'dah 1445 AH corresponding to 10 May 2024 AD, - And Article (21 bis "A") of Amiri Decree No. 15 of 1959 regarding the Kuwaiti Nationality Law , and its amending laws, - And Decree No. 84 of 2024 concerning Ministerial Substitutions and Delegations , and its amending decrees, - And based on the recommendation of the Acting Minister of Interior , Has decided:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The Kuwaiti citizenship certificate shall be withdrawn from those mentioned below, and Kuwaiti citizenship shall be withdrawn from anyone who acquired it through dependency on the holder of that certificate: 1. Rifai Khalaf Awad Salem Al-Shammari . 2. Kamal Khalaf Awad Salem Al-Shammari . 3. Rashid Khalaf Awad Salem Al-Shammari . 4. Bardan Khalaf Awad Salem Al-Shammari . 5. Matloub Khalaf Awad Salem Al-Shammari . 6. Saeed Mohammed Hadi Dhaiban Al-Ajmi . 7. Masoud Mohammed Hadi Dhaiban Al-Ajmi . 8. Hadi Mohammed Hadi Dhaiban Al-Ajmi . 9. Mohammed Awad Jabran Mohammed Al-Ajmi . 10. Muthakkar Manahi Ayed Shtran Al-Ajmi . 11. Mubarak Manahi Ayed Shtran Al-Ajmi . 12. Ahmed Gholoum Haji Hassan .

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The Minister of Interior shall implement this decision, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette . Acting Prime Minister Fahad Yusuf Saud Al-Sabah Issued on: 22 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 20 April 2025 AD Submitted by / Insurance Regulatory Unit Against / Ghazal Insurance Company Bankruptcy Judge In the Name of His Highness the Amir of the State of Kuwait Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah The Supreme Wisdom Bankruptcy Department

chunks.title: Decision
chunks.text_en: On Thursday, 2025/4/10, at the headquarters of the Bankruptcy Department , With the membership of Professor / Zaid Abdul Hadi Al-Attar , Bankruptcy Judge , And in the presence of Professor / Khalid Abdullah Al-Qaisi , Secretary, Regarding Application No.: (23/2024 Bankruptcy Month ) Registered under Automated Number: 240825520 Submitted by: Insurance Regulatory Unit Against: Ghazal Insurance Company After reviewing the documents and the Secretary's letter dated 2025/4/6 requesting the issuance of a decision authorizing him to extract bank statements for Ghazal Insurance Company for the period from 2025/1/1 until the present date, and in accordance with the provisions of the Bankruptcy Law and its Executive Regulations . The facts are summarized in the Bankruptcy Judge's decision issued on 2025/3/16 regarding the Secretary's right to carry out his scope of work in financial and criminal investigation, undertake necessary financial and in-kind settlements, issue arrest warrants and financial and legal prosecution orders, and file lawsuits as referred to in his letter dated 2025/2/16, which was referenced to avoid repetition. Whereas Article 1 of the Bankruptcy Law stipulates that "Precautionary Measures : Procedures taken by the Bankruptcy Court or the Bankruptcy Judge in accordance with the provisions of this Law with the aim of preserving and managing the debtor's assets or the bankruptcy estate and preventing their concealment, including placing seals on the debtor's place of business, appointing a temporary trustee to manage his assets, imposing restrictions on the debtor or any related party in disposing of his assets, or preventing him and any of his board members , or managers , or board members or managers of any company related to the debtor , from traveling for a certain period or until a certain event occurs." And Article (9) of the same Law stipulates that "The Bankruptcy Department shall be responsible for the following: 1 - 2 - 3 - 4- Issuing all decisions within the jurisdiction of the Bankruptcy Judge under this Law , notifying concerned parties thereof, announcing them, and publishing them. 5- Monitoring the debtor's assets and operations, expediting procedures, and taking necessary measures as specified in this Law ...". And Article (32) stipulates that "The Bankruptcy Judge , upon a request submitted to him by the regulatory authority or any interested party , may decide to take any precautionary measures , including placing seals on the debtor's place of business, appointing a temporary trustee to manage his assets, imposing restrictions on him in disposing of his assets, or preventing him and any of his board members or managers from traveling for a certain period or until a certain event occurs. The Bankruptcy Judge , upon a request submitted to him by the debtor , may issue his decision to suspend claims." And Article (1/52) stipulates that "The inspector , in order to carry out the assigned task, may request the testimony of any person without administering an oath, and may also request any information or documents from the debtor or a creditor or units subject to the Central Bank or persons licensed by the Authority or regulatory bodies or any governmental or non-governmental entity." Based on the foregoing, and since the Secretary requested the issuance of a decision authorizing him to extract the bank statements of Ghazal Insurance Company from Kuwait Finance House , Gulf Bank , and Boubyan Bank so that he can complete his scope of work and finalize the financial and criminal investigation procedures and issue the necessary arrest and prosecution orders, and since granting the Secretary's request serves the interest of the creditors and the bankruptcy estate , we therefore decide to issue the decision authorizing him to extract the company's bank statements from the aforementioned banks for the period stated. Therefore, we have decided the following: To authorize the Secretary to extract the following bank statements of Ghazal Insurance Company : 1- Account No. (96237683) at Gulf Bank 2- Account No. (0114334002) at Boubyan Bank 3- The company's accounts at Kuwait Finance House . This is for the period from 2025/1/1 until the present date, and the Bankruptcy Department shall provide the Secretary with a certified true copy of this decision and legally notify the concerned parties thereof. <footer> Secretary Bankruptcy Judge In accordance with Article (8) of the Bankruptcy Law , decisions issued by the Bankruptcy Judge shall be executive instruments according to this Law , and the Bankruptcy Department shall affix the enforcement formula to them, and they shall be enforceable immediately upon their issuance and without notification. </footer>

chunks.title: Regarding the Cancellation of the License for the Electronic Media Outlet named (Al-Rawdatain Electronic Newspaper <orig>جريدة الروضتين الإلكترونية</orig>)
chunks.text_en: Assistant Undersecretary for the Press, Publishing, and Printed Materials Sector Having reviewed, - Law No. 8 of 2016 regarding the Regulation of Electronic Media. - Ministerial Decision No. 100 of 2016 issuing the Executive Regulations for Law No. 8 of 2016 regulating Electronic Media. - Administrative Decision No. 655 of 2022 regarding the Delegation of Certain Competencies. - Electronic Media License No. 2017/189 issued on: 09/11/2017. - The application submitted by the licensee on: 15/04/2025. - The letter from the Director of the Electronic Publishing Department No.: 21 dated: 16/04/2025. - And as required by public interest. Has decided (Article One) - License No. 2017/189 for the electronic media outlet (Al-Rawdatain Electronic Newspaper ) is hereby cancelled by operation of law, based on the request of the licensee, in accordance with the last paragraph, item 8 of Article 16 of the aforementioned Law No. 8 of 2016 regulating Electronic Media. (Article Two) - Concerned parties shall be notified of this decision, and it shall be published on the Ministry's website and in the Official Gazette. Assistant Undersecretary for the Sector of Press, Publishing, and Printed Materials Issued on: 24 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 22 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Regarding the Cancellation of the License for (Alamkashouf <orig>عالمكشوف</orig>) Magazine
chunks.text_en: Assistant Undersecretary of the Ministry of Information for Press, Publishing, and Printed Materials Affairs - Having reviewed Law No. 3 of 2006 regarding Publications and Publishing, and its amendments. - And Ministerial Decision No. 52 of 2006 on the Executive Regulations for the Law of Publications and Publishing and its amendments. - And the letter from the Local Press Department No. 186 dated 15/4/2025, which includes the licensee's request. Has decided (Article One) The license issued for (Alamkashouf ) Magazine is hereby cancelled, based on the request of the licensee, issued on 24/7/2001, in accordance with the last paragraph of Article (14) of the aforementioned Law No. 3 of 2006. (Article Two) The competent authorities shall implement this decision, and it shall be published in the Official Gazette, and concerned parties shall be notified by registered mail with acknowledgment of receipt. Assistant Undersecretary of the Ministry for Press, Publishing, and Printed Materials Affairs Issued on: 24 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 22 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Regarding: Amending Certain Provisions of Ministerial Decision No. 81/1976 on the Executive Regulations of the Traffic Law and its Amendments
chunks.text_en: First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, Having reviewed Decree-Law No. 67/1976 regarding Traffic and its amendments. And upon Ministerial Resolution No. 1976/81 regarding the Executive Regulations of the Traffic Law and its amendments. And upon the letter from the Assistant Undersecretary for Traffic and Operations Affairs No. 79129 dated 2025/3/11 and its attachments. And based on the presentation of the Undersecretary . Decided

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: Article (211 bis) of the aforementioned Ministerial Resolution No. 1976/81 shall be amended to read as follows: The reconciliation amount with the accused in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed, in implementation of the provision of Clause (7) of Article (41) of Decree-Law No. 2025/5 regarding some provisions of Decree-Law No. 1976/67 concerning Traffic, shall be according to the following principles: 1. A sum of seventy Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by no more than twenty kilometers. 2. A sum of eighty Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than twenty kilometers up to thirty kilometers. 3. A sum of ninety Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than thirty kilometers up to forty kilometers. 4. A sum of one hundred Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than forty kilometers up to fifty kilometers. 5. A sum of one hundred and twenty Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than fifty kilometers up to sixty kilometers. 6. A sum of one hundred and thirty Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than sixty kilometers up to seventy kilometers. 7. A sum of one hundred and fifty Kuwaiti Dinars shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than seventy kilometers.

clauses.text_en: A sum of seventy Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by no more than twenty kilometers.
A sum of eighty Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than twenty kilometers up to thirty kilometers.
A sum of ninety Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than thirty kilometers up to forty kilometers.
A sum of one hundred Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than forty kilometers up to fifty kilometers.
A sum of one hundred and twenty Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than fifty kilometers up to sixty kilometers.
A sum of one hundred and thirty Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than sixty kilometers up to seventy kilometers.
A sum of one hundred and fifty Kuwaiti Dinars  shall be paid in case of exceeding the prescribed maximum speed by more than seventy kilometers.

chunks.title: Article (2)
chunks.text_en: The Undersecretary shall implement this resolution, and it shall be effective from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette . First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior Fahad Yusuf Saud Al-Sabah Issued on: 4 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: April 2, 2025 AD

chunks.title: Regarding the Amendment to Ministerial Resolution No. (24) of 2020 Regarding the Formation of the Supreme Committee of the Insurance Unit

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Article One of the aforementioned Ministerial Resolution No. (24) of 2020 shall be amended, with regard to the membership of the representative of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, as follows: - Ms. Marwa Baddah Muslim Al-Humaidan (Representative of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry ).

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this resolution, each within its jurisdiction, and it shall be effective from the date of its issuance and published in the Official Gazette . Minister of Commerce and Industry Khalifa Abdullah Al-Ajeel Issued on: 19 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: April 17, 2025 AD - And Ministerial Decision No. 2024/94 regarding the delegation of certain competencies. - And the letter from the General Department of Nationality and Kuwaiti Travel Documents No. 3419 dated 2/3/2025 and its attachments. Decided

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: The minor / Hussein Hamad Rahman Kadhim Shuwerd - born in Kuwait on 7/7/2018, son of citizen / Ma'souma Mohammed Abbas Abdullah Awad Hussein - from her deceased husband / Hamad Rahman Kadhim Shuwerd - Iraqi national - shall be treated as Kuwaiti citizens until he reaches the age of majority.

chunks.title: Article (2)
chunks.text_en: Those concerned shall implement this decision, and it shall be effective from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette . Undersecretary of the Ministry of Interior Lieutenant General Salem Nawaf Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah

chunks.title: (Decision No. 1653 of 2025)
chunks.text_en: Regarding: Treatment of Minors Undersecretary of the Ministry of Interior - After reviewing Amiri Decree No. 59/15 regarding the Kuwaiti Nationality Law and its amending laws. - And Ministerial Decision No. 2024/94 regarding the delegation of certain competencies. - And the letter from the General Department of Nationality and Kuwaiti Travel Documents No. 3946 dated 16/3/2025 and its attachments. Decided

chunks.title: Article (1)
chunks.text_en: The minor / Hussein Ali Ajeel Hussein Makki - born in Kuwait on 18/4/2009, son of citizen / Maha Mudallah Jureidi Ali Al-Rashidi - from her divorced husband / Ali Ajeel Hussein Makki - illegally residing - shall be treated as Kuwaiti citizens until he reaches the age of majority.

chunks.title: Article (2)
chunks.text_en: Those concerned shall implement this decision, and it shall be effective from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette . Undersecretary of the Ministry of Interior Lieutenant General Salem Nawaf Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah <footer> Issued on: 18 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 16 April 2025 AD </footer> - Decided - **Article One** The requirements and specifications accompanying this decision are hereby adopted for licensing a unit or complex to provide psychological treatment and consultations in the private medical sector. **Article Two** This decision shall be communicated to those concerned for its implementation and shall be effective from the date of its issuance. It shall be published in the Official Gazette, and any decision or text conflicting with its provisions shall be revoked. **Minister of Health** **Dr. Ahmed Abdulwahab Al-Awadhi ** Issued on: 25 Ramadan 1446 AH Corresponding to: 25 March 2025 AD *Requirements and Specifications for Licensing a Unit or Complex for Providing Psychological Treatment and Consultations in the Private Medical Sector* **First: Definitions:** For the purpose of applying the provisions of this regulation, the following words and phrases shall have the meanings indicated opposite each of them: 1- Psychological Treatment and Consultations: Refers to the provision of non-pharmacological "professional psychological services" to individuals, families, groups, public organizations, or private organizations, whether for a fee or without a fee. 2- Professional Psychological Services: Refers to the practical application of scientifically proven psychological principles, methods, or procedures for the purpose of treating or eliminating behavior

clauses.text_en: Psychological Treatment and Consultations: Refers to the provision of non-pharmacological "professional psychological services" to individuals, families, groups, public organizations, or private organizations, whether for a fee or without a fee.
Professional Psychological Services: Refers to the practical application of scientifically proven psychological principles, methods, or procedures for the purpose of treating or eliminating behavior

chunks.title: (Decision No. 1654 of 2025)
chunks.text_en: Regarding: Treatment of Minors **Undersecretary of the Ministry of Interior** - After reviewing Amiri Decree No. 59/15 regarding the Kuwaiti Nationality Law and its amending laws. - And Ministerial Decision No. 94/2024 regarding the delegation of certain competencies. - And the letter from the General Department of Kuwaiti Nationality and Travel Documents No. 963 dated 19/1/2025 and its attachments. Decided **Article (1)** The minor / Aisha Zaidan Khazal Ali - born in Kuwait on 5/9/2010, daughter of citizen / Tarfa Khalaf Thani Saud Al-Shammari - from her divorced husband / Zaidan Khazal Ali , an illegal resident - shall be treated as Kuwaiti citizens until she reaches the age of majority. **Article (2)** Those concerned shall implement this decision, and it shall be effective from the date of its publication in the Official Gazette. **Undersecretary of the Ministry of Interior** **Lieutenant General** **Salem Nawaf Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah ** Issued on: 18 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 16 April 2025 AD provided that, in addition to the licensee, one or more practitioners as stipulated in the workforce clause of this decision shall work in this complex. 3- A license to open a complex for providing psychological consultations shall be granted to a Kuwaiti company whose objectives include the establishment, operation, and management of a facility for providing psychological consultations, provided that it employs a licensed psychologist with at least a first-level practitioner degree in psychotherapy (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the Civil Service Commission decisions), holding a doctorate degree according to the requirements stipulated in this decision, and fully dedicated to working in the psychological consultation complex, in addition to one or more practitioners as stipulated in the workforce clause of this decision. harmful, maladaptive, or undesirable, and promoting personal relationships and guidance in various aspects of life for self-development, improving work, social, and family relationships, increasing quality of life, enhancing personal effectiveness, and improving mental health. The scope of practicing psychotherapy and psychological and social consultations includes all of the following: a- Diagnosis and treatment of psychological problems related to thoughts, feelings, and behavior, as well as psychological symptoms of physical illness, physical injury, or disability. b- Providing non-pharmacological psychotherapy accompanying the treatment of alcohol addiction, dependence disorders, and drug abuse problems. c- Applying psychological scales and assessments, intelligence and personality tests, cognitive abilities, interests, attitudes, and professional and academic aptitudes. d- Education and awareness related to topics of psychological assessment, therapy and consultations, and managing and improving academic and work environments. e- Providing psychological counseling, psychotherapy, psychoanalysis, cognitive behavioral therapy, positive therapy, acceptance and mindfulness therapy, dialectical behavioral therapy, marital and family counseling, applied behavior analysis, or any non-pharmacological therapeutic practices falling under therapy or psychological consultations. f- Measuring neurocognitive abilities, developing and implementing clinical neuropsychological treatment and rehabilitation programs, measurement using biofeedback or neurofeedback devices, and using electroencephalography (EEG) devices. 3- Psychological Consultation Unit: This is a place where psychological consultations are provided by a licensed Kuwaiti psychologist working alone without the presence of other practitioners, within independent spaces. 4- Psychological Consultation Complex: This is a place where psychological consultations are provided by one or more psychologists in addition to one or more practitioners as stipulated in the workforce clause, provided that the complex is within independent, interconnected spaces or on successive floors. Third: Conditions for Granting a License for a Psychological Consultation Unit or Complex: 1- A license to open a psychological consultation unit shall be granted to a licensed Kuwaiti psychologist with at least a first-level practitioner degree in psychotherapy (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the Civil Service Commission decisions), holding a doctorate degree according to the requirements stipulated in this decision, provided that they work alone in the unit without the presence of other psychologists or practitioners. 2- A license to open a psychological consultation complex shall be granted to one or more licensed Kuwaiti psychologists with at least a first-level practitioner degree in psychotherapy (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the Civil Service Commission decisions), provided that they hold at least a Master's degree or a Higher Diploma and have practical experience and clinical training according to the requirements stipulated in this decision. And at the level of Senior Psychotherapist Practitioner at least (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission ). 2- Psychological Researcher : A psychology specialist who conducts clinical or counseling interviews, and whose work is limited to collecting information and performing appropriate psychological assessments for cases as requested by and under the supervision of a licensed psychotherapist. Provided that they hold a Bachelor's degree in Psychology according to the requirements stipulated in this decision, and at the level of Psychological Researcher at least (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission ), provided that they do not provide any clinical psychotherapy services. 3- Applied Behavioral Analyst (ABA): A specialist in understanding, analyzing, and identifying the causes of disordered behavior, who develops and implements an educational program for behavior modification, especially for persons with disabilities, through the application of applied learning techniques. Provided that they hold a Bachelor's degree in Psychology and a professional certificate in Applied Behavior Analysis accredited by the Ministry of Higher Education . And at a level equivalent to a Psychotherapist Practitioner at least (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission ), provided that they do not provide any clinical psychotherapy services. 4- Applied Behavioral Technician: A specialist in applying an educational program for behavior modification, especially for persons with disabilities, through the application of applied learning techniques. Provided that they hold a Diploma in Applied Behavior Analysis accredited by the Ministry of Higher Education . And at a level equivalent to a Therapy Technician at least (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission ), provided that they do not provide any clinical psychotherapy services. 5- Occupational Therapist (Occupational Therapy Specialist) : A specialist in rehabilitation for cases suffering from difficulties in performing daily life tasks or work. They provide therapeutic interventions to enable children with disabilities to actively participate in academic and social life. They assist patients in recovering from injury to regain their skills. They provide support to the elderly who suffer from impaired physical or cognitive abilities. They must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in Occupational Therapy, and be at the level of Occupational Therapy Practitioner at least (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission ), provided that they do not provide any clinical psychotherapy services. 6- Speech and Hearing Specialist : A specialist in providing therapy to individuals suffering from disorders in hearing, communication, and spoken and written language. They must hold at least a Bachelor's degree in Speech and Hearing Therapy at the level of Speech and Hearing Practitioner at least (according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission ), provided that they do not provide any clinical psychotherapy services. 7- Any other relevant health specializations in psychotherapy after obtaining the approval of the Psychiatry Department in the Ministry of Health . 8- Director of the Psychological Complex : If the licensee of the Psychological Complex does not wish to be its Medical Director , they must appoint a Medical Director who will be technically responsible for it and effectively supervise the management of its operations, in accordance with the following controls: a- Must be a psychotherapist at the level of Senior Psychotherapist Practitioner at least and hold a Doctorate degree according to the requirements stipulated in this decision, provided that they are fully dedicated to working in the Psychological Complex . b- Must not have been previously convicted in cases involving breach of honor and trust . c- Their period of residence in the State must not be less than 9 months each year. d- Must not be a Medical Director of any health facility or any of its branches. e- Must have experience in the field of management or the activity of the Complex. f- Obtain prior approval from the Ministry. g- A Deputy Director may be appointed to assist in performing their duties, and in the absence of the Medical Director , a deputy must be assigned, provided that the same conditions apply to them. Fifth: Requirements for Obtaining a Psychotherapist License : General Conditions: An applicant seeking to obtain a license as a psychotherapist to work in the private sector in the State of Kuwait must meet the following: 1- Must hold at least a Master's degree or a Higher Diploma in Psychology or its equivalent, and have completed the academic requirements from educational institutions accredited by the Ministry of Higher Education in the State of Kuwait, with submission of proof of the request for academic equivalency of the scientific certificate from the accredited authorities in the State, and according to the requirements stipulated in this decision. 2- Must have "practical experience" in a health facility or a psychotherapy or psychological counseling facility, and "supervised clinical training." Academic Requirements for Psychotherapists Holding a Doctorate: Obtaining a Doctorate or its equivalent in the field of Psychology, Clinical Psychology, Psychotherapy, Psychological Counseling, Child Life Care, Clinical Social Work program, or a General Doctorate program in Psychology, provided that the academic outcome meets the following: 1- The study program must be an integrated and structured sequence of study within a college or university accredited by the Ministry of Higher Education in the State of Kuwait, It is permissible for a course to be repeated (i.e., the same content) provided it is at a different academic level. Courses that teach other areas of psychology not related to psychological diagnosis and therapy are not considered. 4- Any academic courses in the field of psychotherapy completed through an affiliation system (without being registered within an academic program) may be counted towards fulfilling the requirement of studying academic courses in the field of psychotherapy, provided that the educational institution is recognized by the Ministry of Higher Education in the State of Kuwait .

chunks.title: Practical Experience and Clinical Training for Psychotherapists Holding a Doctorate, Master's, or Higher Diploma
chunks.text_en: A psychotherapist must have "practical experience" and "supervised clinical training" as follows: 1- Must have practical experience of no less than 6 years after obtaining the university qualification (Bachelor's degree), which is equivalent to at least the title of Senior Psychotherapy Practitioner according to the academic qualifications and years of experience stipulated in the decisions of the Civil Service Commission . The duration may be continuous or intermittent, provided it is in the field of psychotherapy from within or outside the State of Kuwait , and practical training or residency (Internship) years during the postgraduate stage are counted within the practical experience years. 2- The practical experience must include "supervised clinical training" for no less than 1000 therapy sessions (session duration 45-60 minutes), conducted face-to-face with cases and supervised by a supervisor holding a doctorate degree and a license in psychotherapy, provided that 25% of these therapy sessions are under direct supervision from the supervisor. Direct supervised clinical training hours during the postgraduate stage are counted as part of the therapy sessions, provided that the number of hours is clearly stated in the academic transcript and mentioned in the course description. 3- The "supervised clinical training" must be conducted in a licensed psychiatric or psychological counseling health facility, or in an entity concerned with education and training in psychotherapy. All reports and case data proving these therapy sessions must be submitted while maintaining the confidentiality of this data, provided that the reports include comments, guidance, supervisor's evaluation, and approval. 4- The supervisor may be a psychiatrist, provided they have experience in psychotherapy and hold at least the rank of Senior Specialist , and work at a university, an institute for medical specialties, or a governmental higher academic institution, provided that 2- The study period for obtaining the doctorate must not be less than 3 full-time academic years, or their equivalent in part-time study, 3- The program must be specialized in Clinical Psychotherapy or Counseling Psychology (PhD in Clinical Psychology or PhD in Counseling Psychology) or a professional doctorate program in Clinical or Counseling Psychology (PsyD or DCounsPsy or DClinPsy) or a general doctorate program in the field of Psychology or Educational Psychology (PhD in Psychology or Doctor of Philosophy in Psychology) or any closely related doctorate program in therapy or counseling psychology such as a clinical social work program, childhood studies, or child life, provided that all the aforementioned programs meet the requirements stated in this clause, 4- The doctoral study system must be based on courses and credit hours, include examinations and evaluation, and be indicated in the academic transcript as actual study within the educational institution through direct instruction with faculty members and with multiple students in the program, with actual presence in the country of study for a period of no less than 75% of the study duration. These courses must include specialized studies in the field of psychology, 5- The applicant must have completed at least 17 different academic courses in the field of psychological diagnosis and therapy during the Bachelor's, Master's, and Doctorate programs, as detailed in the guiding appendix for approved courses in the field of psychological diagnosis and therapy attached to this decision. It is permissible for a course to be repeated (i.e., the same content) provided it is at a different academic level. Courses that teach other areas of psychology not related to psychological diagnosis and therapy are not considered. 6- Any academic courses in the field of psychotherapy completed through an affiliation system (without being registered within an academic program) may be counted towards fulfilling the requirement of studying academic courses in the field of psychotherapy, provided that the educational institution is recognized by the Ministry of Higher Education in the State of Kuwait .

chunks.title: Academic Requirements for Psychotherapists Holding a Master's or Higher Diploma
chunks.text_en: The applicant must hold a Master's degree or Higher Diploma or their equivalent in any of the fields of psychology, educational psychology, child life care, or social work, provided that the following academic outcomes are met: 1- The study program must be an integrated and regular sequence of study within a college or university accredited by the Ministry of Higher Education in the State of Kuwait , 2- The study system must be based on courses and credit hours, include examinations and evaluation, and be indicated in the academic transcript as actual study within the educational institution and through direct instruction 2- In the case of clinical psychotherapy, the clinical interview must be based on building a professional relationship between the therapist and the client, collecting clinical information, and applying appropriate psychological scales for the client's condition, in order to formulate the diagnosis or differential diagnosis, and then develop the treatment plan. When providing services to children, sufficient training for dealing with them must be available. Diagnosis is made by the psychological therapist and is expected to be in accordance with a scientifically proven diagnostic system such as (ICD-11) or (DSM-5TR), while following any updated editions. 3- In the case of psychological counseling, the counseling interview must be based on building a professional relationship between the therapist and the client, collecting information, and applying appropriate psychological scales for the client's condition, and then developing the counseling plan. 4- The necessity of accurate documentation of all diagnostic and therapeutic processes practiced in the unit or complex, along with the necessity of writing a comprehensive initial assessment for each client and keeping it in their file. If there is a need to record the therapy session for documentation within the therapist's training hours or for educational purposes, written consent from the patient must be obtained. 5- It is strictly prohibited for personnel in the psychotherapy unit or complex to direct clients to use any medicinal treatments, supplements, or dietary regimens. Should the matter necessitate it, they must be referred to a specialized and licensed practitioner by the Ministry of Health in the State of Kuwait to provide these medications, nutritional supplements, health products, special foods, or herbal preparations. 6- The practice of any non-invasive therapeutic intervention, such as electrical, magnetic, or ultrasound brain stimulation devices, or any other similar devices, is prohibited unless a permit for their use in the unit or complex has been obtained from the competent authority. 7- The scope of practice for psychological therapists is limited to working under the supervision of a psychological therapist holding a Doctorate degree. Psychological therapists holding a Doctorate degree, in addition to performing all tasks of psychological therapists who do not hold a Master's degree or Higher Diploma, shall also: 1- Supervise therapists working in the facility, if any. 2- Provide supervision services for therapy sessions required to meet the supervised clinical experience requirements for psychological therapists. 3- Approve psychological reports and tests, and write and approve session summaries, provided that reports are free of recommendations. Upon request from the client or their legal representative, the summary must be free of any recommendations with legal or Sharia effect. If any recommendations are requested by the client or their legal representative, they must be referred to the Kuwait Center for Mental Health affiliated with the Ministry for writing these recommendations. Sixth: General Provisions: 1- Psychotherapy offices and psychological consulting companies or psychological and social consulting companies with valid licenses from the Ministry of Commerce are granted a one-year grace period starting from the date of issuance of this decision to regularize their status and fulfill the requirements contained herein. must be accredited by the National Bureau for Academic Accreditation of the State of Kuwait . 5- Faculty members holding a Doctorate degree in one of the fields of psychology or educational psychology who work in a government university, or any higher academic institution accredited by the National Bureau for Academic Accreditation of the State of Kuwait , are exempted from the requirement of practical experience in the field of psychotherapy or the requirement of fulfilling the number of course credits they passed during their studies, provided that they have practical experience in teaching for no less than (5) years or scientific research in one of the courses mentioned in the Guidance Appendix for Approved Courses in the field of psychotherapy. They must also fulfill the requirements for therapy sessions mentioned in this clause under "Supervised Clinical Experience." Academic Requirements for Psychological Researchers Holding a Bachelor's Degree Obtaining a Bachelor's degree in any of the fields of psychology, educational psychology, or child life care, provided that the academic outcome meets the following: 1- The study program must be an integrated and organized sequence of study within an accredited college or university by the Ministry of Higher Education in the State of Kuwait . 2- The study system must be based on a course and credit hour system and include exams and evaluation, as indicated in the academic transcript. The study must be actual within the educational institution and through direct instruction with faculty members and the presence of multiple students in the program, with actual presence in the country of study for a period of no less than 75% of the study duration. These courses must include specialized studies in the field of psychology. Practical Experience and Clinical Training for Psychological Researchers Holding a Bachelor's Degree 1- Must have practical experience of no less than two years after obtaining the university qualification (Bachelor's degree), equivalent to at least the title of psychological researcher or psychotherapy practitioner. The period may be continuous or intermittent, provided it is in the field of psychology from within or outside the State of Kuwait . Sixth: Job Description: Psychological Researcher Holding a Bachelor's Degree 1- Their work is limited to providing the initial clinical interview or initial counseling interview (Initial assessment), which includes collecting information and applying appropriate psychological scales for the client's condition as requested and under the supervision of the licensed psychological therapist. When providing services to children, sufficient training for dealing with them must be available. 2- The necessity of accurate documentation of all information and results of tests and psychological scales performed in the unit or complex. Psychological Therapist Holding a Master's Degree or Higher Diploma 1- Allowed to receive psychological cases that can be treated as outpatient cases. However, cases requiring medical interventions or hospitalization must be referred to the specialized doctor or hospital. This also applies to cases suffering from acute symptoms that pose a danger to the patient themselves or others, such as acute symptoms of substance abuse, active suicidal ideation, or severe agitation. The psychological therapist must emphasize to the patient or their family the necessity of visiting the hospital to receive treatment, along with the necessity of providing a record 3- The practitioner must adhere to the professional ethics and conduct stipulated in Law No. 70 of 2020 , Chapter Three, Article (9). 4- The decisions regulating the technical requirements that must be met in health facilities and by practitioners apply to the health facility and its practitioners. Guidance Appendix for Courses in the Field of Psychotherapy: Courses or study topics that are studied under the semester system (courses). The semester system refers to a study term of approximately 4 months during the spring or autumn semester, and approximately two months during the summer semester, or their equivalent in the annual system. This must include tests and evaluation, and be recorded in an academic Transcript. The study must be actual, conducted within the educational institution, through direct instruction with faculty members, and with multiple students in the program. The course content must include the following topics:

chunks.title: Core Courses in Therapy
chunks.text_en: - Clinical Psychology - Psychotherapy - Psychopharmacology - Ethical and Professional Issues in Clinical Practice - Evidence-based Interventions. - Theories in Clinical Psychology - Group Psychotherapy - Addiction psychotherapy - Techniques of Psychotherapy - Time-Effective Treatment

chunks.title: Core Courses in Counseling
chunks.text_en: - Counseling theories - Group Counseling - Career counseling - Multicultural counseling - Marriage and Family Counseling - Addiction counseling - School psychology or counseling - Counseling for Children and adolescents - Psychology of Stress - Stress and Trauma

chunks.title: Courses in Psychological Testing and Measurement
chunks.text_en: - Personality assessments - Clinical assessments

chunks.title: Courses in Psychotherapy Methods
chunks.text_en: - Psychoanalytic Practice - Cognitive Behavior Therapy CBT Theories and Practice - Dialectic Behavior Therapy DBT Theories and Practice - Mindfulness Therapy

chunks.title: Courses in Clinical Psychological Services
chunks.text_en: - Social Work with Families - Group work. - Group work dynamics - Family Therapy - Clinical Practice with Couples - Anti-Oppressive Practice in Families and Communities - Theories and Practice with specific populations (LGBT, Children, Adults, Older Adults..etc)

chunks.title: Core Courses in Psychology
chunks.text_en: - Cognitive Neuroscience - Biological Psychology - Physiological Psychology - Affective Neuroscience - Psychology of Individuals with Special Needs - Lifespan Psychopathology - Health Psychology - Systems and History of Psychology or Clinical Psychology - Diversity Issues in Psychology or Clinical Psychology - Personality Psychology or Clinical Personality Psychology - Developmental Psychology or Child Clinical Psychology - Professional Orientation or Clinical Professional Orientation

chunks.title: Core Courses in Diagnosis
chunks.text_en: - Abnormal Psychology - Mental disorder diagnosis - Clinical Interview

chunks.title: Other Courses
chunks.text_en: - Any course shows evidence that its content is related to psychotherapy.

chunks.title: Regarding the Regulation of Work between the Internet and Communications Gateway of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and Local Telecommunications and Internet Networks
chunks.text_en: The Chairman of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority : - Having reviewed Law No. (37) of 2014, establishing the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And Decree No. (43) of 2025, regarding the termination of work with Decree No. (144) of 2022, concerning the formation of the Authority's Board of Directors, - And the Esteemed Council of Ministers' Decision No. (993) of 2015, issuing the Executive Regulations for Law No. (37) of 2014, establishing the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And the Decision of the Minister of State for Communications Affairs No. (18) of 2025 issued on 2025/3/18, - And the letter from His Excellency the Minister of State for Communications Affairs No. 27 dated 2025/1/8 regarding the request for approval of the special decision between the Authority's Internet and Communications Gateway and local telecommunications networks, - And the Decision of the Authority's Board of Directors No. (1/2025) taken at its meeting No. (377) held on 2025/1/22, approving the draft decision regarding the regulation of work between the Authority's Internet and Communications Gateway and local telecommunications networks, - And based on the exigencies of work. Has decided:

chunks.title: Definitions
chunks.text_en: * **The Authority**: The Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority . * **The Internet and Communications Gateway of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority **: The communications and information technology systems of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority that enable it to connect local and international telecommunications and internet networks. This includes internet network systems and wireless communication networks with the aim of regulating and supervising the telecommunications sector. * **Telecommunications and Internet Company Networks**: The internet communication systems used locally in the networks of telecommunications and internet companies licensed as local telecommunications and internet service providers, and licensed to manage, establish, or operate a telecommunications network or internet service to provide communication services to the public. This includes information or content providers whose services are provided via telecommunications and information technology networks. It defines the communication data traffic of any person party to a contract with a telecommunications operator.

chunks.title: Regarding the Tariff for International Transit Circuits
chunks.text_en: The Chairman of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority : - Having reviewed Law No. (37) of 2014, establishing the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And Decree No. (1) of 2025, regarding fees and financial costs for the utilization of public facilities and services, - And Decree No. (43) of 2025, regarding the termination of work with Decree No. (144) of 2022, concerning the formation of the Authority's Board of Directors, - And the Esteemed Council of Ministers' Decision No. (993) of 2015, issuing the Executive Regulations for Law No. (37) of 2014, establishing the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And the Esteemed Council of Ministers' Decision No. (377) taken at its meeting No. (12/2025) - (47) held on 2025/3/26, regarding the project to stimulate demand for transit circuits, - And the Decision of the Minister of State for Communications Affairs No. (18) of 2025 issued on 2025/3/18, - And the approval of the Authority's Board of Directors at its meeting No. (11) of 2024 dated 2024/10/10, - And the Decision of the Chairman of the Authority's Board of Directors No. (2022/67) dated 2022/4/19, - And the Decision of the Chairman of the Authority's Board of Directors No. (2023/58) dated 2023/2/22, - And based on the exigencies of work. Has decided:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: Monthly Tariff for International Transit Circuits in Kuwait | Circuit Capacity | STM64 (10G) | 100G | |:---|:---|:-----| | Rental Value | KWD 458 | KWD 1,832 |

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: Tariff for International Transit Circuits in Kuwait for 15-Year Commitment with Upfront Payment | Circuit Capacity | STM64 (10G) | 100G | |:---|:---|:-----| | Rental Value | KWD 54,111 | KWD 216,446 |

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: Work with Decision No. (67) of 2022 regarding the tariff for transit circuits and the Data Hub (Data HUP) shall cease, effective from the end of the service provider's subscription.

chunks.title: Article Four
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this decision, each within its jurisdiction. It shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall come into force from the date of its issuance. All provisions contrary to or inconsistent with this decision shall be repealed. Chairman of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority Azbi Jaber Al-Sabah <footer> Issued on: April 24, 2025 AD </footer># Decision No. (74 / 2025)

chunks.title: Regarding the Tariff for International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for Internet Services via International Submarine and Terrestrial Cables and the Interconnection Tariff – Retail Sale
chunks.text_en: The Chairman of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority : - Having reviewed Law No. (37) of 2014, establishing the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And Decree No. (1) of 2025, regarding fees and financial costs for the utilization of public facilities and services, - And Decree No. (43) of 2025, regarding the termination of work with Decree No. (144) of 2022, concerning the formation of the Authority's Board of Directors, - And the Esteemed Council of Ministers' Decision No. (993) of 2015, issuing the Executive Regulations for Law No. (37) of 2014, - And the Esteemed Council of Ministers' Decision No. (377) taken at its meeting No. (47) – (12/2025) held on 2025/3/26, regarding the project to stimulate demand for capacities, - And the Decision of the Minister of State for Communications Affairs No. (18) of 2025 issued on 2025/3/18, - And the approval of the Authority's Board of Directors at its meeting No. (11) of 2024 dated 10/10/2024, - And the Decision of the Chairman of the Authority's Board of Directors No. (17) of 2021 issued on 2021/1/25, - And based on the exigencies of work. Decided

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The following terms – wherever they appear in this Resolution – shall have the meanings indicated opposite each of them: - Existing Capacities: The total registered capacity subscriptions for each service provider at the time of issuance of this Resolution. - Additional Capacities: Capacities allocated to the service provider in excess of the existing capacities.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: Resolution No. (17) of 2021 of the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Authority regarding the tariff for International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for internet services via international submarine and terrestrial cables and the interconnection tariff shall remain in effect for calculating existing capacity subscriptions.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: The tariff for the rental value of additional capacity subscriptions shall be: Firstly: For International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for internet services For 10G STM64 capacity if there is a desire to commit for one year general for obtaining telecommunications and internet services.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: All telecommunications companies and those licensed to manage, establish, or operate a telecommunications network, or internet service to provide communications to the public and information or content providers, must connect the network they use to the Internet and Communications Gateway of the Public Authority for Communications and Information Technology in accordance with the privacy protection standards determined by the Authority.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: The Authority, through the connection of the Internet and Communications Gateway of the Public Authority for Communications and Information Technology and the networks of telecommunications or internet companies, shall undertake to enhance the security protection of telecommunications and internet networks, ensure the application of the provisions stipulated in relevant laws, regulations, and resolutions, guarantee the implementation of security policies for telecommunications, information technology, and internet networks, and activate their safe use by ensuring the application of guidelines and rules established by the Authority. Through this, it can regulate and supervise the telecommunications sector with the aim of improving it and providing protection for users' interests, and ensure that these licensed companies take all necessary technical measures to block any materials, websites, programs, or other content that violate the laws in force in the State of Kuwait , and anything that affects public order and public morals. This is in accordance with the provisions of Article 3, paragraphs (c) and (sh) of Law No. 37 of 2014, referred to.

chunks.title: Article Four
chunks.text_en: Telecommunications and internet companies are obligated, when connecting their network gateway to the Internet and Communications Gateway of the Public Authority for Communications and Information Technology , to adhere to all specifications, standards, and procedures set by the Authority in this regard, pursuant to Law No. 37 of 2014, referred to, and its executive regulations, and all laws in force in the State of Kuwait .

chunks.title: Article Five
chunks.text_en: Without prejudice to the legally prescribed penalties, the Authority has the right, in the event of a proven violation of the provisions of this Resolution, to take the measures stipulated in Article 64 of Law No. 37 of 2014, referred to.

chunks.title: Article Six
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this Resolution, each within its jurisdiction, from the date of its issuance, and it shall be published in the Official Gazette . <footer> Chairman of the Public Authority for Communications and Information Technology, Acting Athebi Jaber Al-Sabah Issued on: 24 April 2025 AD </footer>

chunks.title: Regarding the Tariff for Leased International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for Internet Services via International Submarine and Terrestrial Cables and Interconnection Tariff – Long-Term Commitments
chunks.text_en: **Chairman of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority :** - Upon review of Law No. (37) of 2014, regarding the establishment of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And Decree No. (1) of 2025, regarding fees and financial costs for the utilization of public facilities and services, - And Decree No. (43) of 2025, regarding the termination of work with Decree No. (144) of 2022, concerning the formation of the Authority's Board of Directors, - And the esteemed Council of Ministers' Resolution No. (993) of 2015, issuing the Executive Regulations for Law No. (37) of 2014, regarding the establishment of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority and its amendments, - And the esteemed Council of Ministers' Resolution No. (377) adopted at its meeting No. (47) – (12/2025) held on 26/3/2025, regarding the project to stimulate demand for capacities, - And the Minister of State for Communications Affairs' Resolution No. (18) of 2025 issued on 18/3/2025, - And the approval of the Authority's Board of Directors at its meeting No. (11) of 2024 on 10/10/2024, - And the Chairman of the Authority's Board of Directors' Resolution No. (17) of 2021 issued on 25/1/2021, - And based on the exigencies of work. **Has Decided**

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The tariff for the rental value of long-term commitments shall be: First: For International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for Internet services with a capacity of (10G), in case of a desire to commit for a period of (15) years with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (Two Million, Six Hundred Thirty-Five Thousand, Six Hundred Twenty-Seven Dinars and Six Hundred Ten Fils) KWD 2,635,627.610. Second: For Interconnection Circuits – with a capacity of (10G) STM64, in case of a desire to commit for a period of (15) years with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (Two Million, One Hundred Ninety-Six Thousand, Three Hundred Fifty-Six Dinars and Three Hundred Forty Fils) KWD 2,196,356.340.

clauses.text_en: For International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for Internet services with a capacity of (10G), in case of a desire to commit for a period of (15) years with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (Two Million, Six Hundred Thirty-Five Thousand, Six Hundred Twenty-Seven Dinars and Six Hundred Ten Fils) KWD 2,635,627.610.

For Interconnection Circuits – with a capacity of (10G) STM64, in case of a desire to commit for a period of (15) years with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (Two Million, One Hundred Ninety-Six Thousand, Three Hundred Fifty-Six Dinars and Three Hundred Forty Fils) KWD 2,196,356.340.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: Resolution No. (17) of 2021 of the Chairman of the Authority's Board of Directors regarding the tariff for leased circuits (IPLC) for Internet services via international submarine and terrestrial cables and interconnection tariff shall remain in effect.

chunks.title: Article Three
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this Resolution, each within its respective jurisdiction. It shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall come into force from the date of its issuance. Anything that contradicts or conflicts with the provisions of this Resolution shall be repealed. one with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (One Hundred Thirty Thousand, Eight Hundred Forty-Eight Dinars) KWD 133,848. Second: For Interconnection Circuits – with a capacity of (10G) STM64, in case of a desire to commit for a period of one year with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (One Hundred Eleven Thousand, Five Hundred Forty Dinars) KWD 111,540.

clauses.text_en: For Interconnection Circuits – with a capacity of (10G) STM64, in case of a desire to commit for a period of one year with the rental value tariff paid as a single upfront payment, it shall be (One Hundred Eleven Thousand, Five Hundred Forty Dinars) KWD 111,540.

chunks.title: Article Four
chunks.text_en: First: The tariff specified in Article Three of this Resolution shall apply in accordance with the provisions of this Resolution and shall be effective for a period of three months from the date of its issuance. Second: The tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution shall apply to additional subscriptions, not existing ones. Third: The tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution is available on condition of continuous renewal and non-cancellation of any existing capacity subscription for International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for Internet services or interconnection circuits from the date of issuance of this Resolution. Fourth: The continued application of the tariff for leased circuits (IPLC) for Internet services via international submarine and terrestrial cables and interconnection tariff for existing capacity subscriptions at the time of issuance of this Resolution shall be in accordance with Resolution No. (17) of 2021. Fifth: The provisions of this Resolution shall apply on condition that the additional capacity subscriptions, as per the tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution, are used for sale to end-users, whether individuals or companies (retail sale) – and it is prohibited to sell them to companies holding an Internet service provider or presenter license in accordance with Ministry of Communications Resolution No. (657) of 2012 and the Law of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority , and the regulations and resolutions issued in this regard.

clauses.text_en: The tariff specified in Article Three of this Resolution shall apply in accordance with the provisions of this Resolution and shall be effective for a period of three months from the date of its issuance.
The tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution shall apply to additional subscriptions, not existing ones.

The tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution is available on condition of continuous renewal and non-cancellation of any existing capacity subscription for International Private Leased Circuits (IPLC) for Internet services or interconnection circuits from the date of issuance of this Resolution.

The continued application of the tariff for leased circuits (IPLC) for Internet services via international submarine and terrestrial cables and interconnection tariff for existing capacity subscriptions at the time of issuance of this Resolution shall be in accordance with Resolution No. (17) of 2021.

The provisions of this Resolution shall apply on condition that the additional capacity subscriptions, as per the tariff mentioned in Article Three of this Resolution, are used for sale to end-users, whether individuals or companies (retail sale) – and it is prohibited to sell them to companies holding an Internet service provider or presenter license in accordance with Ministry of Communications  Resolution No. (657) of 2012 and the Law of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority , and the regulations and resolutions issued in this regard.

chunks.title: Article Five
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this Resolution, each within its respective jurisdiction. It shall be published in the Official Gazette and shall come into force from the date of its issuance. Anything that contradicts or conflicts with the provisions of this Resolution shall be repealed. <footer> Acting Chairman of the Communications and Information Technology Regulatory Authority Athebi Jaber Al-Sabah Issued on: 24 April 2025 CE </footer>

chunks.title: Regarding the Declaration of the Federation of Specialized Environmental Institutions and Companies
chunks.text_en: First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior and Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Public Authority for Manpower : * Having reviewed Law No. (6) of 2010 regarding Labor in the Private Sector and its amendments, and the administrative decisions in force. * And Decree-Law No. (109) of 2013 regarding the establishment of the Public Authority for Manpower . * And Decree No. (153) of 2022 regarding the supervision of the Public Authority for Manpower . * And the founding documents deposited with the Public Authority for Manpower . * And based on the exigencies of work interest. *** Has Decided ***

chunks.title: Article One:
chunks.text_en: The Federation of Specialized Environmental Institutions and Companies is hereby declared.

chunks.title: Article Two:
chunks.text_en: This decision shall be effective from the date of its issuance and shall be published along with a summary of the Articles of Association in the Official Gazette . First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior Fahad Yusuf Saud Al-Sabah Issued on: 18 Shawwal 1446 AH Corresponding to: 16 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Federation of Specialized Environmental Institutions and Companies <orig>اتحاد المؤسسات والشركات البيئية المختصة</orig>
chunks.text_en: The Constituent General Assembly met on 2/5/2025, and the founders agreed to establish the Federation of Specialized Environmental Institutions and Companies . Its Articles of Association stipulate the following: * Holders of licenses to practice environmental consulting, environmental services, and environmental activities that meet the conditions specified in Article 5 of these Articles, and who wish to join, shall be accepted into the Federation's membership by a decision of the Board of Directors. * The Federation's fiscal year begins from the start of January to the end of December of each year. * The Federation's Ordinary General Assembly shall convene during the first quarter following the end of the preceding year. * The Federation's Board of Directors shall consist of (5) members elected by the General Assembly by secret ballot for a term of (4) years. * The members of the Board of Directors shall elect from among themselves, at their first meeting, an executive body consisting of a Chairman, a Vice-Chairman, a Secretary, a Treasurer, and a Member. * The bylaws specify the powers of the Ordinary and Extraordinary General Assemblies , the powers of the Board of Directors , and the registers maintained by the Federation.

chunks.title: Regarding the Renewal of Registration of Mr. Ali Abdul Rahman Ibrahim Al-Hassawi <orig>علي عبد الرحمن ابراهيم الحساوي</orig> in the Special Register of Auditors
chunks.text_en: Having reviewed: * Law No. (7) of 2010 regarding the Establishment of the Capital Markets Authority and the Regulation of Securities Activities, its Executive Regulations, and their amendments; * And the registration certificate of Auditor Mr. Ali Abdul Rahman Ibrahim Al-Hassawi No. (0022 / 2013 / EA) issued on 10/05/2025; * And the request of Mr. Ali Abdul Rahman Ibrahim Al-Hassawi to renew his registration as an auditor in the special register at the Capital Markets Authority ; * And based on Decision No. (4) of 2025 regarding the amendment of final approval powers for the work procedures of the Supervision Sector, issued on 12/01/2025. Has decided the following:

chunks.title: Article One
chunks.text_en: The registration of Auditor Mr. Ali Abdul Rahman Ibrahim Al-Hassawi in the special register of auditors at the Capital Markets Authority shall be renewed for a period of three years, renewable, effective from 10/05/2025.

chunks.title: Article Two
chunks.text_en: The competent authorities shall implement this decision, each within its jurisdiction, and it shall be published in the Official Gazette . Head of Supervision Sector Ziyad Yaqoub Yusuf Al-Fulaij Issued on: 21 April 2025 AD

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The owners of Yaghi Al-Lubnaniya Company for Pies, Pastries, and Sweets ## Company Under Liquidation have submitted a request to the Department of Partnerships at the Ministry to carry out: Dissolution and Liquidation of the Company Legal Entity: One-Person Company Anyone with an objection is requested to submit it to the Court of First Instance within thirty days from the date of publication of this announcement, in accordance with the usual procedures for filing a lawsuit. The submission of an objection shall result in the continued obligation of the joint partners, in the case of a general partnership or a simple limited partnership only, towards this creditor, until a judicial ruling is issued on their objection.

chunks.title: The Amiri Diwan <orig>الديوان الأميري</orig>
chunks.text_en: The Amiri Diwan announces the extension of tender No. (D.A/129/2024-2025) for the license to exploit, manage, and operate an investment site at Al-Shaheed Park (Phase Three) - a local healthy restaurant kiosk (A6). Therefore, those wishing to participate, from companies or institutions specialized in this field, should apply to obtain the terms and conditions booklet and tender documents during official working hours from (The Amiri Diwan – Bayan Palace – Administrative Building – First Floor – Zone 7 – Tenders and Follow-up Department). This is during official working hours, starting from the date of publication of the announcement in the Official Gazette (Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ), for a non-refundable fee of (150 K.D.) only one hundred and fifty Kuwaiti Dinars (K-Net payment only) for the booklet. Owners of small and medium enterprises are exempted from these fees (upon submission of the relevant certificate), from 8:30 AM until 1:00 PM, taking into account the following: 1. The last deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Thursday, 1/5/2025. No bids will be accepted after this deadline. 2. Bids must be submitted in a sealed envelope to the Tenders and Follow-up Department – at Bayan Palace – Administrative Building – First Floor – Zone 7. 3. Submission of a preliminary bond of not less than (2%) of the bid value in favor of the Amiri Diwan in the form of a letter of guarantee or a certified check from one of the Kuwaiti banks, valid throughout the bid validity period, which is (90) days. 4. Submission of a valid national labor percentage compliance certificate. 5. A letter of authorization and a copy of the civil ID for the company representative to purchase the tender document. 6. This tender is indivisible, and alternative offers are not permitted. 7. The bidder must submit a letter accompanied by a copy of the registration certificate with the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 8. The bid must remain valid for (90) days from the closing date, with the Amiri Diwan reserving its right to extend or renew this period for the duration(s) it deems appropriate to complete the award procedures. 9. Companies must be registered with the Amiri Diwan (Amiri Diwan website). https://pnsd.da.gov.kw/ar/ The tender is subject to Circular No. (1) of 2023 regarding tenders in public entities. For inquiries: or a letter of guarantee certified by one of the local banks in the State of Kuwait . The submitted bond must be valid for 90 days from the date of opening the envelopes. - Any bid not accompanied by the original preliminary bond, or if the bond value or duration is less than required, will be excluded. - All companies participating in the practice are invited to attend the opening of envelopes at the time and place specified in the table above. Notes: 1. The following conditions apply to those submitting a bid for this practice: - Must be registered with the Central Agency for Public Tenders as well as the Ministry of Awqaf and Islamic Affairs . - Submission of a valid 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , stating that there are no suspensions on the company's files regarding government contracts registered with the Authority. 2. All bid documents must be submitted on a (CD) or non-copyable electronic flash drives (3 copies), in addition to submitting hard copies of the bid format, price schedules, and the original preliminary bond. 3. The tendering of practices and tenders by the Ministry of Awqaf and Islamic Affairs and any other works announced by the Ministry can be followed via the following link: https://tenders.awqaf.gov.kw

chunks.title: Ministry of Health <orig>وزارة الصحة</orig>
chunks.text_en: Announcement The Ministry of Health announces the offering of Practice No.: P.M/S/2026/2025/7 for equipping 28 vehicles with sirens and warning lights – First Responder Project, according to the general and special conditions specified in the practice documents, which can be obtained from the Ministry's website (www.moh.gov.kw), for a non-refundable payment of (75 K.D.) only seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars . It is necessary to attach a letter from the Public Authority for Manpower when submitting the booklet, stating compliance with the National Labor Support Law according to Law No. 19 of 2000 and Cabinet Resolution No. 1104/Fifth of 2008 regarding the support and encouragement of national labor. Please note that the closing date for the practice is at 1:00 PM on Monday, 12/05/2025, at the bid box in the General Office of the Ministry of Health (Sulaibikhat – Ministry of Health Building – First Floor – Procurement Department – Practices Section). Bids are valid for (90) days from the closing date, and the preliminary guarantee is (2%) only two percent of the bid value, valid throughout the bid validity period.

chunks.text_en:
Regarding the Issuance of Practice No. (3/2025-2026) Rental of (7) various 2025 model vehicles for the Audit Bureau without a driver The Audit Bureau announces the issuance of Practice No. (3/2025-2026) in accordance with the terms and specifications contained in the practice documents. Practice Tender Details for Audit Bureau Vehicle Rental | Issuance | Closing | Price | Guarantee | |:------|:-------|:-----|:--------| | 2025/04/27 | 2025/05/12 | 75 | 2% of the total bid value as an initial security deposit for 90 days |
The preliminary meeting will be held at 10:30 AM on Sunday, 4/5/2025, at the Audit Bureau headquarters. The technical meeting for opening envelopes will be held at 11:00 AM on Tuesday, 13/5/2025, at the Audit Bureau headquarters. The practice documents can be obtained from the Supplies Department at the Bureau's headquarters located in (Shuwaikh Administrative - Street 102) during official working hours, and payment is made via K-Net device; Bids must include the initial security deposit, whether in the form of letters of guarantee or certified checks from the bank, bearing the name of the submitting company or establishment, with the Audit Bureau as the beneficiary. It is required to attach (a certificate from the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the practitioner's file regarding government contracts, a clearance certificate issued by the Ministry of Electricity, Water, and Renewable Energy , and a certificate of payment of subscriptions to the Public Institution for Social Security ) with the bids, otherwise the bid will be considered void. Note: Follow the official Audit Bureau website (WWW.SAB.GOV.KW) for any updates to the practice. For inquiries: Tel: 22011433 - 22011432 Email: na.alroumi@da.gov.kw Undersecretary for Financial, Administrative, and Personnel Affairs Notification from the General Secretariat of the Council of Ministers regarding the amendment of the practice closing date to be Monday, 2025/5/5 instead of Tuesday, 2025/4/22. For inquiries, please contact the Contracts and Tenders Control : Switchboard 1800008, extension 2723 Inquiries can be sent to the email: h.albanwan@cmgs.gov.kw

chunks.title: Held on Wednesday Morning
chunks.text_en:
25 Shawwal 1446 AH – Corresponding to 23 – 04 – 2025 AD The Central Agency for Public Tenders held its session No. (2025/37) at nine o'clock on Wednesday morning, corresponding to 23 – 04 – 2025.
