chunks.title: Publication / Preliminary Meeting Minutes and Appendix
Preliminary Meeting
chunks.text_en: 10:00 AM on Sunday, 11-05-2025, at the General Services Department - Al-Shuwaikh - Plot 155 - next to First Car Agency . * The tender documents will be published on the Authority's website for review before proceeding to purchase the documents. * The final time for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on the day specified in the above announcement for depositing bids. * Bidders and practitioners must adhere to the instructions regarding the submission and deposit of bids on the CD available on the Authority's website WWW.CAPT.KW. * The cash equivalent mentioned above shall be paid by certified check or K-Net card. * Companies / bidders wishing to submit inquiries or clarifications, etc., must adhere to submitting them to the concerned entity at the time and place specified above in the table for the preliminary meeting (inquiry session). No attention will be paid to any inquiries or clarifications submitted to the Authority. * All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD, except for the bid format and initial security, which must be submitted as hard copies. * Indivisible. * A valid certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage issued by the Public Authority for Manpower must be submitted with the bid. * The bidder must attach a valid "To Whom It May Concern" certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower stating that there is no suspension on the bidder's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority due to one of the violations mentioned in Article 10 of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the private sector, noting that bids that do not include this certificate will be excluded. * This announcement is considered an invitation for interested bidders to attend, as interested companies have the right to attend the bid opening session. * Interested companies wishing to attend must coordinate with the Bid Opening Department before the scheduled bid opening session and bring the following documents: 1. Copy of the Civil ID. 2. Copy of the signature authorization for the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 3. If someone other than the authorized signatory attends, a written letter from the authorized signatory at the Central Agency for Public Tenders must be presented, authorizing whomever they deem fit to attend the session. * Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempt from the fees prescribed for purchasing documents, in accordance with the text of Article (39) of Law No. 74/2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. 49/2016. * Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises wishing to bid must attach a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Development and Care , confirming their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise. * This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents. * For more information, please visit our website WWW.CAPT.GOV.KW.

chunks.title: Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders <orig>الجهاز المركزي للمناقصات العامة</orig> Announcement
chunks.text_en:
Regarding the issuance of Tender No. W.SH.J. 2024-2025/6 Cleaning, Internal Transportation, and Waste Disposal Works for the Ministry of Social Affairs Upon the request of the Ministry of Social Affairs Tender documents can be obtained from the Central Agency for Public Tenders . Tender Details and Submission Requirements | Tender | Deadline for Bid Submission (Closing Date) | Price | Guarantee | |:---|:---|:---|:---| | 2025/04/27 | 2025/05/27 | 1000 | 62,000 K.D. valid for 90 days in favor of the Central Agency for Public Tenders |
- Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempt from the fees prescribed for purchasing documents, in accordance with the text of Article (39) of Law No. 74/2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. 49/2016. - Small and Medium Enterprise owners wishing to bid for the tender must attach with their offer a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Care and Development , confirming their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise with the Fund. - This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents. - For more information, please visit our website WWW.CAPT.GOV.KW - All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD, with the exception of the bid format and preliminary bond, which must be submitted as hard copies. - Indivisible - A valid certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage, issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , must be submitted with the bid. - The bidder must attach a valid 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , stating that there is no suspension on the bidder's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority due to any of the violations mentioned in Article 10 of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the Private Sector . Bids that do not include this certificate will be disqualified. - This announcement serves as an invitation for interested bidders to attend, as interested companies have the right to attend the bid opening session. - Interested companies wishing to attend must coordinate with the Bid Opening Department before the scheduled date of the bid opening session and bring the following documents: 1. Copy of the Civil ID . 2. Copy of the signature authorization for the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 3. Regarding Tender No. RFP-2098109 Kuwait Export Crude and Jurassic Crude Storage Facilities and Upgrading of Existing Export Network JLO EXPORT FACILITIES&UPGRADING OF EXISTING EXPORT NETWORK - Kuwait Oil Company – The Central Agency for Public Tenders wishes to draw the attention of bidders that a second site visit will be conducted specifically for the locations COM) Email to (FAQALLAF@KOCKW.COM) and a copy to (HAKaram@kockw.com) within a maximum of seven (7) days from the date of the site visit, noting that the gathering point will be in the Al-Ahmadi (North) Tank Farm Security Gate. Therefore, this notification was necessary. Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders The Central Agency for Public Tenders Announces the invitation to public tender No.: (MEW / 18 / 2023-2024) Subject: Maintenance and Repair Works for XLPE Power Cables up to 4000 kv in middle Area Based on the request of Ministry of Electricity & Water and Renewable Energy The public tender documents can be obtained from the Central Agency for Public Tenders, according to the following: Tender Details | Detail | Value | |:-------|:------| | Tender announcement date | 2025 / 4 / 20 | | Tender closing date | 2025 / 5 / 20 | | Tender document Fees | 1000 K.D | | Bid bond value | 45000 K.D valid for (90) days | | Bid bond in favor of | The Central Agency for Public Tenders | | Pre-tender meeting (inquiry session) Time | 10 A.M | | Pre-tender meeting (inquiry session) Date | 30 / 4 / 2025 | | Pre-tender meeting (inquiry session) Location | Shuwaikh Industrial – Infront of the Fire station – Maintenance cables Land Administration and Overhead Lines. |
* Closing date for submitting bids is no later than (1:00) PM of the tender announcement date. * Bidders wishing to participate in this tender must abide by the CAPT's instructions published on the CAPT's website– www.capt.gov.kw * Bids must be submitted on a compact disc (CD/ DVD) with (5) non-modifiable copies, with an exception to the form of tenders and the bid bond to be submitted as hard copies. * indivisible. * Tenders documents fees must be paid by certified cheque or Knet card. * Inquiries and clarifications by bidders who paid the tender documents fees must be provided to the relevant public authority at the time and place specified above with a schedule for the pre- tender meeting (inquiry session), any inquiries or clarifications submitted directly to the CAPT will not be consider. * A valid certificate of national manpower quota issued by the Public Authority for Manpower must be submitted with the bid, and a ‘To Whom It may concern certificate’ stating no hold applicable on the bidder file. * Owners of small and medium enterprises must be present a valid support documents issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprises prior to the settlement of the tender document fees and to be submitted with their bids. * This announcement is considered as an invitation to bidders to attend the bids envelops opening session after prior coordination with the CAPT before the designated date presenting the following documents: * Copy of the civil ID. * A copy of the signature approval form issued by of the Central Agency for Public Tenders. * Letter to be signed by the authorize individual at Central Agency for Public Tenders to attend the session. * This announcement is considered to be a part of the public tender documents.
Secretary General Central Agency for Public Tenders * The public tender documents will be published on CAPT's website, for review prior to purchasing. Once the tender document fees been paid refund is not acceptable unless the tender is canceled in accordance with the provisions of Article (55) of the Public Tenders Law and its amendments.

Announcement of Tender Invitation RFP No.: 2107454 Group of Flow Lines for Manifolds and Gathering Lines in North Kuwait - INCOMING SUB – GROUP MANFOLDS AND GROUP TRUNK LINES (MGT) IN NORTH KUWAIT AREA As per the request of Kuwait Oil Company Tender documents can be obtained from the Central Agency for Public Tenders . Tender Schedule and Requirements | Tender | Scheduled Date for Bid Submission (Closing Date) | Price | Guarantee/Bond | |:---As per the request of Kuwait Oil Company Tender documents can be obtained from the Central Agency for Public Tenders .
Tender Details and Key Dates | Tender | Scheduled Date for Bid Submission (Closing Date) | Price | Guarantee / Bond | |:------|:---|:-------|:--------| | 2025/04/27 | 2025/07/27 | 10000 | 850,000 K.D. valid for 90 days in favor of the Central Agency for Public Tenders | | Preliminary Meeting (Inquiry Session) | 9:00 AM on Tuesday, 27-05-2025, via Microsoft Teams | | |
The tender documents will be published on the Agency's website for review before proceeding to purchase the documents. - The final time for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on the day specified in the above announcement for bid submission. - Bidders and practitioners must adhere to the instructions regarding the submission and deposit of bids on the CD available on the Agency's website WWW.CAPT.GOV.KW. - The aforementioned cash payment shall be made by certified check or K-Net card. - Companies / bidders wishing to submit inquiries or clarifications must adhere to submitting them to the concerned entity at the time and place specified above in the table for the preliminary meeting (inquiry session). No attention will be paid to any inquiries or clarifications submitted to the Agency. - All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD, with the exception of the bid form and preliminary bond, which must be submitted as hard copies. - Not divisible. - A valid certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage, issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , must be submitted with the bid. - The bidder must attach a valid "To Whom It May Concern" certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , stating that there is no suspension on the bidder's file regarding government contracts registered with the Authority due to any of the violations mentioned in Article 10 of Law No. 6 of 2010 concerning work in the private sector. Bids that do not include this certificate will be disqualified. - This announcement serves as an invitation for interested bidders to attend, as interested companies have the right to attend the bid opening session. - Interested companies wishing to attend must coordinate with the Bid Opening Department before the scheduled bid opening session and bring the following documents: 1. Copy of the Civil ID . 2. Copy of the signature authorization for the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 3. If someone other than the authorized signatory attends, a written letter from the authorized signatory at the Central Agency for Public Tenders must be provided, authorizing the attendee to be present at the session. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempt from the prescribed fees for purchasing documents, in accordance with Article (39) of Law No. 74 of 2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. 49 of 2016. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises wishing to bid must attach a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Development and Care , confirming their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise. - This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents. - For more information, please visit our website WWW.CAPT.GOV.KW Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders Announcement Regarding the Issuance of Tender No. RFP-2140425 Provision of Comprehensive Maintenance and Operational Support Services for Jurassic Production Facilities (JPF-1, 2 & 3) in North Kuwait COMPREHENSIVE MAINTENANCE AND OPERAATIONAL SUPPORT FOR JURASSIC PRODUCTION FACILITIES (1.2&3) AT NORTH KUWAIT Companies wishing to attend must coordinate with the Bid Opening Department before the scheduled day of the bid opening session and bring the following documents: 1. Copy of the Civil ID . 2. Copy of the signature authorization from the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 3. In case of attendance by a person other than the authorized signatory, a written letter of authorization from the authorized signatory at the Central Agency for Public Tenders must be presented, delegating whomever they deem fit to attend the session. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempted from the prescribed fees for purchasing documents, in accordance with the text of Article (39) of Law No. 74/2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. 49/2016 . - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises wishing to bid for the tender must attach with their bid a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Development and Care , including their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise with the fund. - This announcement is considered an integral part of the tender documents. - For more information, please visit our website at WWW.CAPT.GOV.KW Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders

chunks.title: Notice
chunks.text_en: Regarding Tender No. 2140425-RFP - Provision of Comprehensive Maintenance and Operational Support Services for Jurassic Production Facilities (JPF-1, 2 & 3) in North Kuwait COMPREHENSIVE MAINTENANCE AND OPERAATIONAL SUPPORT FOR JURASSIC PRODUCTION FACILITIES (1.2&3) AT NORTH KUWAIT - Kuwait Oil Company The Central Agency for Public Tenders wishes to draw the attention of bidders to proceed with receiving the document related to the aforementioned tender from the headquarters of the Central Agency for Public Tenders . Hence, this notice was deemed necessary. Secretary General of the Central Agency for Public Tenders Therefore, colleges, universities, and training centers wishing to participate in this qualification must register on the official website of the General Directorate of the National Guard (www.kng.gov.kw) to purchase the qualification documents for an amount of (75/-) K.D. (seventy-five Kuwaiti Dinars only), non-refundable. Payment is to be made through the National Guard's website using the (K-NET) service. Furthermore, a preliminary meeting will be held at 10:00 AM on Wednesday, (07/05/2025), at the General Directorate of the National Guard - Colonel / Faisal Al-Jareed Building. Participants, whether present or not, will be bound by all decisions, recommendations, or clarifications contained in the meeting. Participating colleges and universities must also print the minutes of the preliminary meeting from the General Directorate of the National Guard's website (www.kng.gov.kw), approve it, and attach it with the bid documents. Please note that the deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Monday, (26/05/2025). Note: An authorization letter from the college/university to attend the meeting and the purchase receipt. For any inquiries, please call the switchboard at (24999999) extension: (24999858) Fax: (66769-66983) (66270006).

chunks.title: Extension of Closing Date
chunks.text_en:
The Public Institution for Social Security announces the practice No. (PIFSS/P/27/2024) regarding the provision of primary and secondary global internet network service and the provision of technical support and maintenance services for it at the Public Institution for Social Security , which was previously announced on 30/03/2025 in Issue No. (1732). The new closing date will be Thursday, 08/05/2025, instead of 24/04/2025, with emphasis that bids must be submitted at the Institution's headquarters, and the final deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM. Acting Director General Tender No. A.Y. 2024/11 Completion of Works for Al-Hudayb Mosque in Al-Sabahiya Area – Block (1) The Ministry of Islamic Affairs announces the offering of Tender No. A.Y. 2024/11 for the completion of works for Al-Hudayb Mosque in Al-Sabahiya Area – Block (1), in accordance with the following terms and data: Tender Details for Mosque Completion Works | Tender Subject | Requesting Entity | Practice Fees Value | Closing Date | Public Envelope Opening Meeting | |:---|:---|:---|:---|:---| | Completion of Works for Al-Hudayb Mosque in Al-Sabahiya Area – Block (1) | Ministry of Islamic Affairs | 150 K.D. | 11/5/2025 AD | 2:00 PM on Sunday, 4/5/2025 AD, in the meeting room attached to the office of the Assistant Undersecretary for Administrative and Financial Affairs on the 6th floor. |
1. Tender documents can be obtained starting from Sunday, 27/4/2025 AD, and on subsequent days, during official working hours. 2. From the Tenders Department of the Financial Affairs Department – 6th Floor, Ministry of Islamic Affairs building in Al-Riqqa area. 3. The fees for purchasing tender documents shall be deposited into the account of the Ministry of Awqaf and Islamic Affairs / Mosques Sector at Kuwait Finance House (Account No.: 111010026507), with a copy of the deposit receipt to be presented upon collection of the tender documents. 4. A preliminary meeting regarding the tender will be held in the meeting room of the Engineering Affairs Department at 10:00 AM on Sunday, 4/5/2025 AD. 5. The deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Sunday, 11/5/2025 AD. Bids received after this deadline will not be considered. 6. Bids shall be submitted to the Tenders Department accompanied by a preliminary bond of 2000 K.D. , in the form of a certified check or an approved letter of guarantee from one of the local banks in the State of Kuwait . The submitted bond must be valid throughout the validity period of the bid (90 days). 7. All documents must be submitted on a non-copyable CD, except for the bid form and the preliminary bond form , which must be submitted as hard copies.

chunks.title: Notes:
chunks.text_en: 1. Must have recent prior experience (within the past five years) similar to the tender works, with a value of no less than (100,000 K.D. ) one hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars . 2. The company's capital must be no less than (100,000 K.D. ) one hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars .

chunks.title: Regarding the Offering of Auction No. 71 / 2024 – 2025
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Information announces the offering of Auction No. 71 / 2024 – 2025 for the investment of sites within the Ministry of Information complex for the sale of beverages and food products of international specifications. This is in accordance with the terms and technical specifications contained in the auction documents, which can be purchased via the Ministry of Information's website using the barcode attached to the announcement, starting from Sunday, 27/4/2025, for a non-refundable fee of (30 K.D. ) thirty Kuwaiti Dinars only. Offers must be submitted to the Procurement Department – Ground Floor – Ministry of Information – Al-Soor Street , inside sealed envelopes, accompanied by a preliminary bond of no less than 2% and no more than 5% of the bid value, in the form of a certified check or an unconditional, unreserved, and irrevocable letter of guarantee issued by one of the local banks in the State of Kuwait in favor of the Ministry of Information , valid for the bid's validity period (90 days). The envelope must be sealed with red wax and the auction number, as indicated above, must be written on the outside, while avoiding any mention of the bidder's name or any indication thereof. The deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM on Thursday, 22/5/2025. 4. The Ministry of Information will hold a preliminary meeting on Sunday, 4/5/2025, at 11:00 AM at the office of the Director of the Public Services Department in Shuwaikh Industrial Area , Block 1, Street 14, Public Services Department of the Ministry of Information , to answer inquiries regarding the auction. Anyone who has purchased the auction documents may attend the meeting in person or through a representative. All information recorded in the meeting minutes shall be considered an integral part of the auction documents and shall be binding on bidders. The Ministry will circulate the questions, inquiries, and their approved responses to all bidders before the bid closing date. 5. The aforementioned cash payment shall be made via K-Net card . 6. Companies wishing to submit inquiries, clarifications, etc., must adhere to submitting them to the Ministry of Information at the time and place specified for the preliminary meeting. 7. The bid must be submitted in a single sealed envelope containing all documents filled, signed, and stamped by the bidder. * All documents must be submitted on 3 non-copyable CDs, except for the (bid form and preliminary bond ), which must be submitted as hard copies. * Owners of small and medium enterprises are exempt from the prescribed fees for purchasing documents. * The practitioner may attach to their bid a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Development and Care , indicating their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise.

chunks.title: The bidder must meet the following conditions:
chunks.text_en: 1. Must have recent prior experience (within the past five years) similar to the tender works, with a value of no less than (100,000 K.D. ) one hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars . 2. The company's capital must be no less than (100,000 K.D. ) one hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars .

chunks.title: Notes:
chunks.text_en: * The auction is indivisible, and alternative offers will not be accepted. * This announcement is considered an integral part of the auction documents. * To obtain a copy, the practitioner must register and pay the fees through the website: https://eservice.media.gov.kw/purchase <image_caption>QR Code for Ministry of Information E-Service Purchase</image_caption> <image> { "description": "QR code linking to the Ministry of Information's e-service purchase portal.", "type": "qr_code", "data": "https://eservice.media.gov.kw/purchase" } </image>

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: A request has been submitted by the owners of Outfit Company for Receiving and Delivering Clothes, which is under liquidation. A request has been submitted to the Department of Partnerships at the Ministry to carry out the dissolution and liquidation of the company. Legal Entity: One-Person Company . Anyone with an objection is requested to submit it to the General Court within thirty days from the date of publication of this announcement, in accordance with the usual procedures for filing a lawsuit. The submission of an objection shall result in the continued obligation of the joint partners, in the case of a general partnership or a simple limited partnership only, towards this creditor, until a judicial ruling is issued on their objection.

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: A request has been submitted by the owners of Al-Dana Al-Zarqa Company for Mobile Food Sales, which is under liquidation. A request has been submitted to the Department of Partnerships at the Ministry to carry out the dissolution and liquidation of the company. Legal Entity: One-Person Company . Anyone with an objection is requested to submit it to the General Court within thirty days from the date of publication of this announcement, in accordance with the usual procedures for filing a lawsuit. The submission of an objection shall result in the continued obligation of the joint partners, in the case of a general partnership or a simple limited partnership only, towards this creditor, until a judicial ruling is issued on their objection.

chunks.title: Held on Wednesday, 2025/4/16
chunks.text_en:
The Ordinary General Assembly of the shareholders of Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait (K.S.C.P.) held its annual meeting at 11:00 AM on Wednesday, 2025/4/16, on the fifteenth floor of the Bank's head office. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Talal Mohammed Reda Yousef Behbehani – Chairman of the Board of Directors – and was attended by: **First:** a) By direct attendance: Shareholders representing – in person and by proxy – 1,608,060,776 shares, constituting 64.92% (direct attendance percentage) of the total shares of the Bank after excluding treasury shares. b) By electronic attendance: Shareholders representing 643,774,023 shares, constituting 25.99% (electronic attendance percentage) of the total shares of the Bank after excluding treasury shares. Thus, the total number of shares of shareholders present directly and electronically is: 2,251,834,799 shares, with a total voting percentage of 90.910% of the Bank's total issued and paid-up capital after excluding treasury shares. **Second:** Ms. Sheikha Adnan Al-Fulaij (from Al-Aiban & Al-Osaimi & Partners – a member of Ernst & Young), and Mr. Ali Bader Al-Wazzan (from Deloitte & Touche – Al-Wazzan & Partners ), the Bank's auditors for the fiscal year ended 2024/12/31. **Third:** Mr. Khalid Abdullah Al-Omari , representative of Kuwait Clearing Company . **Fourth:** Ms. Manal Jassim Al-Hamad – Representative of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . The meeting secretariat was handled by Mr. Fawzi Thanyan Ali Al-Thanyan – Director General of Board Affairs (Board Secretary). After Mr. Talal Mohammed Reda Yousef Behbehani , Chairman of the Board of Directors (Chairman of the Assembly), announced the availability of the legal quorum for the Ordinary General Assembly meeting, the Chairman of the Assembly opened the meeting, welcoming the esteemed shareholders and thanking them for attending. Thereafter, the Ordinary General Assembly proceeded to discuss the items on its agenda and issued the following resolutions regarding them: Thereafter, the Extraordinary General Assembly proceeded to discuss the items on its agenda and issued the following resolutions regarding them: 1. Approval of increasing the Bank's authorized, issued, and fully paid-up capital from KWD 249,310,667.500 (Two Hundred Forty-Nine Million, Three Hundred Ten Thousand, Six Hundred Sixty-Seven Kuwaiti Dinars and 500 Fils) distributed over 2,493,106,675 shares, with a par value of 100 Fils per share, to KWD 261,776,200.800 (Two Hundred Sixty-One Million, Seven Hundred Seventy-Six Thousand, Two Hundred Kuwaiti Dinars and 800 Fils) distributed over 2,617,762,008 shares. The value of the increase is KWD 12,465,533.300 (only Twelve Million, Four Hundred Sixty-Five Thousand, Five Hundred Thirty-Three Kuwaiti Dinars and 300 Fils), representing 5% of the capital, by issuing 124,655,333 new shares to be distributed to shareholders registered in the Bank's records as free bonus shares at a rate of 5% (five shares for every one hundred shares), as detailed in the following timeline: Free bonus shares at a rate of 5% (five percent) will be distributed to shareholders registered in the Bank's records as of the end of the entitlement date, which is Sunday, May 11, 2025. The distribution of bonus shares to the accounts of eligible shareholders will commence on Tuesday, May 13, 2025. The Board of Directors is authorized to amend the aforementioned timeline for implementing the General Assembly's resolution to distribute bonus shares if the registration procedures are not completed eight business days before the entitlement date or for any other reasons. The value of this increase will be covered from the 2024 profits, and the Board of Directors is authorized to dispose of any fractional shares arising therefrom as it deems appropriate. - This item was approved by direct vote by all shareholders present in person, with 100% of the direct attendance percentage, which was 64.92%. - Regarding electronic voting, this item was approved by electronic vote by 24.158%, not approved by electronic vote by 0.000%, and abstained from voting on this item by electronic vote (without stating reasons) by 1.833%, all calculated from the total shares of the Bank's capital after excluding treasury shares. 2. Approval was granted to amend Article (6) of the Articles of Association and Article (5) of the Bylaws of Al Ahli Bank of Kuwait as follows: Text before amendment: 2025-04-27", "issue_number": 1736, "volume_number": 71, "document_type": "official_gazette", The company shall be entitled to conduct the aforementioned activities in the State of Kuwait and abroad, as principal or agent. The company may also engage in activities similar, complementary, essential, or related to its stated purposes. Furthermore, the company may have an interest in or participate in any manner with entities engaged in similar activities or those that may assist it in achieving its objectives in Kuwait or abroad. It may establish, participate in, acquire, or merge with these entities. 7% of the company's capital. The record date will be determined by the Board of Directors for shareholders registered in the company's records at a later time after obtaining official approvals. 1. Approval of the amendment to Article No. (6) of the Articles of Incorporation and Article No. (5) of the Articles of Association regarding the company's capital, to become as follows: Text before amendment: The authorized, issued, and paid-up capital of the company was set at KWD 8,500,000 (eight million five hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), distributed over 85,000,000 shares (eighty-five million shares), with a par value of 100 fils per share, and all shares are cash shares. Text after amendment: The authorized, issued, and paid-up capital of the company was set at KWD 3,000,000 (three million Kuwaiti Dinars ), distributed over 30,000,000 shares (thirty million shares), with a par value of 100 fils per share, and all shares are cash shares. 2. Approval of the Board of Directors' recommendation to reduce the number of Board members from 7 members to 3 members. 3. Approval of the Board of Directors' recommendation to reduce the number of Board members from 7 members to 3 members. 4. Approval of the amendment to Article No. (14) of the Articles of Association regarding the number of Board members, to become as follows: Text before amendment: The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors consisting of 7 members. The entity authorized to appoint representatives to the Board of Directors shall appoint members in proportion to its shareholding in the company, and the General Assembly shall elect the remaining members by secret ballot. Text after amendment: The company shall be managed by a Board of Directors consisting of 3 members. The entity authorized to appoint representatives to the Board of Directors shall appoint members in proportion to its shareholding in the company, and the General Assembly shall elect the remaining members by secret ballot. Chairman of the Board of Directors After the Chairman of the session verified and reviewed all documents and proxies of those present in person and by proxy, he announced the validity of the quorum for the Extra-ordinary General Assembly meeting of Kuwaiti Company for Shareholders K.S.C. (Closed) , with the attendance of shareholders representing 7,524,630 shares (seven million five hundred twenty-four thousand six hundred thirty shares), representing an attendance rate of 75.246% of the total capital shares of 10,000,000 shares. - Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar , Chairman of the session, welcomed the esteemed shareholders present, and then the meeting began with the presentation and discussion of the agenda items and voting on them. First: Appointment of a Liquidator for the Company: The esteemed shareholders present, owning 7,524,630 shares, representing 75.246% of the capital shares, unanimously approved the following: 1- The appointment of Mr. Hussein Abdullah Ahmed Jawhar (Kuwaiti nationality), Civil ID No. 259051502046, as a voluntary liquidator for Kuwaiti Company for Shareholders K.S.C. (Closed) , with all powers granted according to the provisions of the applicable laws in the State of Kuwait . 2- Calculation of the liquidator's fees at KWD 500 (five hundred Kuwaiti Dinars ) for the entire liquidation period. Second: Determining the Liquidation Period for Two Years: Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar – the company's liquidator and Chairman of the session – clarified that the company has ongoing lawsuits by/against it that are still proceeding in the courts of the State of Kuwait . - The esteemed shareholders present unanimously approved setting the liquidation period for two years. At the conclusion of the meeting, the Chairman of the session extended his thanks to the esteemed attendees. The proceedings of the Extra-ordinary General Assembly meeting concluded at 12:15 PM on the same date of its convening. Legal Liquidator – Chairman of the Session

chunks.title: Minutes of the Extra-ordinary General Assembly Meeting for the Fiscal Year Ended December 31, 2024 Minutes of the Extra-ordinary General Assembly meeting of Salhia Real Estate Company K.S.C.P. for the Year Ended on 31/12/2024
chunks.text_en: The Extra-ordinary General Assembly of shareholders of Salhia Real Estate Company K.S.C.P. held its twenty-Seventh meeting for the fiscal year ending on December 31, 2024, at 11:30 AM on Thursday, April 17, 2025, at Courtyard Marriott Hotel – Hall 4 – Third Floor. The meeting was conducted both physically and electronically. The Extra-ordinary General Assembly of shareholders of Salhia Real Estate Company K.S.C.P. for the fiscal year ending on 2024/12/31 was held at 11:30 AM on Thursday, 2025/04/17, at the Courtyard Marriott Hotel – Hall 4 – Third Floor, where the meeting was conducted both physically and electronically. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Faisal Abdul Mohsen Al-Khatrash – Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors, and attended by: – - The company's auditor, Mr. Bader Adel Al-Abduljader from Al-Aiban & Al-Osaimi & Partners Ernst & Young . - The representative of Kuwait Clearing Company , Mr. Mohammed Bouhadi . After the Chairman of the Assembly announced the completion of the legal quorum with the attendance of shareholders representing 72,412,301 shares out of 85,000,000 shares, i.e., 85.19%, the Chairman of the meeting opened the session, welcoming the esteemed attendees and thanking them for accepting the invitation to attend the meeting. Then, the Extra-ordinary General Assembly began discussing the agenda as follows: 1. Approval of the Board of Directors' recommendation to reduce the authorized, issued, and paid-up capital of the company from KWD 8,500,000 (eight million five hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) to KWD 3,000,000 (three million Kuwaiti Dinars ), by an amount of KWD 5,500,000 (five million five hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), which is equivalent to 64.7% of the company's capital, due to excess capital. The amount shall be returned to shareholders, each according to their proportion, provided that it is for shareholders registered in the company's records up to the date of the General Assembly meeting.

chunks.title: Minutes of the Extra-ordinary General Assembly Meeting (Amended)
chunks.text_en: The Extra-ordinary General Assembly of shareholders of First Education Company for the fiscal year ending on 2024/09/30 was held in the meeting room at the headquarters of First Education Company at 1:00 PM on Wednesday, 2025/03/19, chaired by Mr. Mohammed Ghaith Al-Tayyar – Chairman of the Board of Directors – and attended by the company's auditor, Mr. Ammar Al-Hassoun from Al-Qatami & Al-Aiban & Partners (Grant Thornton) , and Mr. Ali Mohammed Al-Balqasi , representative of the Sharia Supervisory Authority from Al-Mashoura Wal Ri'aya for Sharia Consultations Company . After the Chairman of the Assembly announced the completion of the legal quorum with the attendance of shareholders representing 72,412,301 shares out of 85,000,000 shares, i.e., 85.19%, the Chairman of the meeting opened the session, welcoming the esteemed attendees and thanking them for accepting the invitation to attend the meeting. Then, the Extra-ordinary General Assembly began discussing the agenda as follows: 1. Approval of the Board of Directors' recommendation to reduce the authorized, issued, and paid-up capital of the company from KWD 8,500,000 (eight million five hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) to KWD 3,000,000 (three million Kuwaiti Dinars ), by an amount of KWD 5,500,000 (five million five hundred thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), which is equivalent to 64.7% of the company's capital, due to excess capital. The amount shall be returned to shareholders, each according to their proportion, provided that it is for shareholders registered in the company's records up to the date of the General Assembly meeting. General Assembly . 54 54 shares presented in the meeting, % abstaining from 93.4with voting. Khairat, the Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors, presided over the meeting, with the attendance of the Company's Auditor, Mr. Bader Adel Abduljader from Ernst & Young, Al-Aiban and After the announcement of the quorum for the Extraordinary General Assembly, the attendees discussed the item on the agenda, which was to consider the future of the company. The attendees unanimously decided by 100% the following: - 1- Liquidation of the company. 2- Appointment of Mr. Abdullah Abdul Latif Al-Tuwaiti , Kuwaiti national, holding civil ID number: 261011200177, as liquidator for the company and determination of his fees. 3- The liquidation period is one year, renewable. The minutes were closed, and the meeting concluded at 10:00 AM. Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors

chunks.title: Under Liquidation - Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting held on 09/04/2025
chunks.text_en: The Extraordinary General Assembly of Al-Ra'eda Projects Company K.S.C.C. (Closed) – Under Liquidation, was held on Wednesday, 09/04/2025, at 01:30 PM at the headquarters of Industrial and Financial Investments Company – in Sharq area – Block 5 – Jaber Al-Mubarak Street – Commercial Real Estate Building – Fourth Floor. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar , the company's liquidator, and attended by: - Mr. Ahmed Hamdy Nabih Abdel Nabi , representative of Al-Waha Audit Office - After auditing and reviewing all documents related to the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting and the proxies of the attendees, both in person and by proxy, the Chairman of the session announced the completion of the legal quorum for the Extraordinary General Assembly, as shareholders representing 10,000,000 shares, constituting (100%) of the capital amounting to K.D. 1,000,000, were present in person and by proxy. - The Chairman of the session welcomed the attendees and requested to begin discussing the agenda items and voting on them as follows: **First / Extension of Liquidation Period for One Year:** - Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar – Chairman of the session, explained the reasons for not completing the liquidation due to lawsuits filed against the company, and there is no possibility to estimate the time period that the procedures for ending these cases may take. - Based on the foregoing, the attending shareholders unanimously approved by 100% the following: 1. Extension of the company's liquidation period for one year from the date of annotation in the Commercial Register. 2. Mandating the company's liquidator to complete the procedures with the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . **Second / Appointment of Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar as Liquidator for the Company:** - The attending shareholders unanimously approved by 100% the appointment of Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar as liquidator for the company for one year. **Third / Determination of Liquidator's Fees:** - Mr. Hussein Abdullah Jawhar – Chairman of the session, proposed that the liquidation be without fees, and the attending shareholders unanimously approved by 100%

chunks.title: Minutes of the Extraordinary General Assembly Meeting
chunks.text_en: The Extraordinary General Assembly meeting of Al-Ghunaim Commercial Company Limited (K.S.C.C.) (Closed) was held at 11:00 AM on Sunday, 20/4/2025, at the company's headquarters located in Shuwaikh Industrial Area , Plot 41-44. The meeting session was chaired by Mr. Abdul Ghani Khalid Al-Ghunaim , in his capacity as Chairman of the Board of Directors. Also present were: (1) Mr. Sabah Khalid Al-Ghunaim . Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors (2) Mr. Abdul Ghani Abdul Ghani . Chief Executive Officer (3) Mr. Khalid Abdul Ghani Al-Ghunaim . Member (4) Mr. Nasser Saud Al-Manea . Representing Gulf Securities Depository Company (5) Saleh Abdul Wahab Abu Al-Naja . Secretary Shareholders holding 1,371,250 shares in person and 1,429,000 shares by proxy, with a paid-up capital of K.D. 3,000,000 (only three million Kuwaiti Dinars) and a legal quorum percentage of 93.34%. After the announcement of the legal quorum for the validity of the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting, the Chairman of the Board of Directors opened the session, welcoming the attendees, and then began presenting the items on the Extraordinary General Assembly's agenda for discussion of the following: **Text Before Amendment** Amendment of Article (6) of the Articles of Association, which stipulated the following before the amendment: Without prejudice to the provisions of Decree-Law No. 1/2016 concerning Companies and its amendments and executive regulations, the purposes for which the company was established are: – (1) Food catering (562100). (2) Food catering management (562912). (3) Restaurant management (562911). (4) Food service management (562910). (5) Providing ready-made and cold meals (561094). (6) Collecting surplus food and beverages, preserving them, and redistributing them (562914). (7) Providing beverages (coffee shop) (563011). (8) Cold and hot beverages (563015). (9) Buying and selling land and real estate for the company's account only (681011). (10) Buying and selling shares and bonds for the company's account (649917). (11) Investing financial surpluses in financial portfolios by investing them in financial portfolios managed by specialized companies and entities (649918). **Text After Amendment and the purposes for which the company was established are to carry out the following:** **Adding the following activities to the company's activities** (1) Restaurant activity (561010) "Approved" (2) Cafeteria (561020) "Approved" (3) Snacks (561034) "Approved" (4) Pies, sweets, and pastries (463061) "Approved" The meeting concluded at 11:30 AM. Chairman of the Board of Directors The Ordinary General Assembly meeting concluded at 01:45 PM on the same date of the meeting. Company Liquidator

On Wednesday, April 16, 2025, at 12:30 PM, at the company's headquarters located in Sharq Area - Plot 5 - Ahmed Al-Jaber Street - Baram Tower - 7th Floor / M, the Extraordinary General Assembly meeting of Al-Sharqiya Educational Services Company (K.S.C.C.) was held. In attendance was the company's auditor, Mr. Taheri Bharmal from PricewaterhouseCoopers Al-Shatti & Partners (by proxy), with an attendance rate of 97.929% of the shareholders. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Othman Ayman Boudi - Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Sharqiya Educational Services Company (K.S.C.C.) . The agenda of the Extraordinary General Assembly was discussed, and the following was approved: 1. Approval of the reduction of the company's issued capital from KWD 6,000,000 (Six Million Kuwaiti Dinars ) to KWD 5,500,000 (Five Million Five Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), by an amount of KWD 500,000 (Five Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ), due to the non-payment of the issued capital. 2. Approval of the amendment of Article No. (6) of the Articles of Association and Article No. (5) of the Bylaws , as follows: - **Text Before Amendment:** The company's authorized and issued capital is set at KWD 6,000,000 (Six Million Kuwaiti Dinars ), distributed over 60,000,000 shares, with each share valued at 100 fils , and all shares are cash shares. Director General of the General Department of Nationality and Kuwaiti Travel Documents The General Department of Nationality and Travel Documents announces that the individuals whose names are listed below claim to have lost their Kuwaiti passports. Therefore, anyone who finds any of them is requested to hand them over to the Department within two weeks from the date of publication of this announcement in the Official Gazette, otherwise, these individuals will be granted Kuwaiti nationality certificates according to the established procedures. chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en:
The Ministry of Health (Medical Warehouses Department - Drug Control ) announces the offering of the following tenders: Ministry of Health Medical Supply Tenders | No. | Description | Fees | Closing Date | |:----|:------|:-------|:---| | 1 | Pills for treating urinary tract conditions for the needs of all hospitals | 150 | 20 days from the date of announcement or more | | 2 | Antiviral capsules for the needs of all hospitals | 150 | 20 days from the date of announcement or more | | 3 | Injections for treating osteoporosis for the needs of all hospitals | 1000 | 20 days from the date of announcement or more | | 4 | Pills for treating blood pressure for the needs of all departments in health centers and hospitals | 150 | 30 days from the date of announcement or more |
Bids shall be placed in the bid box at the Medical Warehouses Department , provided that the bids remain valid for a period of (90 days) from the closing date. Companies wishing to participate in the tender must meet the following conditions: 1. The national company must be registered with the Medical Warehouses Department in the suppliers' register. 2. The national company must specialize in the medical field and be certified by an accredited certificate from the Chamber of Commerce and Industry . 3. When submitting two offers, two booklets must be purchased with different receipts. 4. Attach a valid product registration certificate from the Department of Registration and Control of Medical and Herbal Drugs , or proof of submission of the certificate, and a copy of the manufacturer's and material's registration in the suppliers' register at the Medical Warehouses Department within the contents of the submitted bid (specific to Drug Control ). 5. Provide samples of new materials, whether a new product requested for the first time or from a new manufacturer, for the specialized control (Drugs – Medical Supplies). 6. Attach a national labor support percentage certificate. 7. Tender documents can be obtained from the Ministry of Health's official website. 8. The fee for purchasing the booklet can be paid through the website to be attached with the tender documents. 9. Submit price offers in a sealed envelope, including the tender documents, according to the closing date specified on the Ministry of Health's official website.

chunks.title: Minutes of the Meeting of the Births and Deaths Committee <orig>لجنة المواليد والوفيات</orig>
chunks.text_en: Held on 8/4/2025 After reviewing the requests submitted for amending data in the birth and death records, the committee decided to publish the following in the Official Gazette (Kuwait Today / Al-Kuwait Al-Youm ). If the committee does not receive any objection within fifteen days from the date of publication of this announcement, the amendment will be made in the records, and birth or death certificates will be issued according to the following amendment:

chunks.title: Minutes of the Meeting of the Births and Deaths Committee <orig>لجنة المواليد والوفيات</orig>
chunks.text_en:
Held on 8/4/2025 The Births and Deaths Committee , stipulated in Article 15 of Law No. 69/36 and formed by Ministerial Resolution No. 2021/1650 , met. The Births and Deaths Committee , stipulated in Article 15 of Law No. 69/36 and formed by Ministerial Resolution No. 2021/1650 , met. * Dr. / Head of the General Medical Council - Member * Mr. / Head of Research and Opinion Department - Member * Mr. / Head of Deaths Department - Member * Mr. / Head of Records Department - Member * Mr. / Legal Researcher - Member Ms. / Alia Al-Saffar acted as the committee's secretary. The committee reviewed requests from number ((143)) to number ((155)) in the attached list, pertaining to data amendments in birth and death records for unregistered birth and death applications. Please note that the sale procedures will take place at the Sales Center - Al-Ardiya Al-Hirafiya . Note: - Full payment via K-Net on the same day. Those wishing to purchase are requested to attend at the time and place specified above. Supervisor of the Sales Center Filed by: 1- Manal Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 2- Mawaheb Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 3- Hanan Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 4- Rabia Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 5- Mona Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 6- Salma Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 7- Noura Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . Against: 1- Raed Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . 2- Mohammed Abdullah Mohammed Al-Habib . Property Description: As stated in the Municipal Property Description Certificate: Property of Document No. 2015/5696 located in Al-Shuhada area - Plot (119) , Block No. (5) , Plan M/37698 , with an area of 400 m² , to be sold by public auction for a base price of K.D. 800,000 (Eight Hundred Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** There is a violation in the property, which is an addition to the building on the first floor and the roof. ** And according to what was stated in the expert's report. The property subject of the lawsuit located in Al-Shuhada area, Block (5) , Street (509) , House (1) , Automated Number (15793365) , and the inspection was carried out as follows: 1- The disputed property is compliant in terms of general and specific specifications with Document No. (5696) and has an area of (400) square meters . 2- The disputed property is a house located on an inner corner, overlooking two streets, with a neighbor on the opposite side and from the rear side. 3- The property's door was knocked several times, but no one opened it. Therefore, the property was inspected from the outside as follows: - The disputed property consists of a ground floor, a first floor, a second floor, and a roof. - The disputed property has (3) entrances: a main entrance, a side entrance from the left, and a side entrance from the right. - The disputed property has several shaded parking spaces.
Those wishing to purchase must attend at the time and place specified above. For any further details, please refer to the Sales Center - Al-Ardiya Al-Hirafiya during official working hours. Sales Center Annex
The cladding of the disputed property is "Al-Simja" . Mr. Expert of Knowledge at the Administration took the necessary notes in preparation for estimating the property's value. Secondly: Auction Conditions: Firstly: The auction shall commence at the basic price of the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: Whoever's bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees immediately upon the sale session's conclusion. Thirdly: If the bidder whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by a deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session, and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. In this session, no bid not accompanied by a full deposit of its value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any decrease in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and registration fees for ownership, the expenses of the enforcement procedures amounting to 200 K.D. , legal and expert fees, and the costs of advertising and publishing the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are responsible for them, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Alert: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." President of the General Court 4- Aisha Hamad Abdullah Al-Salal . 5- Latifa Hamad Abdullah Al-Salal . Property Description: (According to the attached description certificate) Property Document No. 2022/11069 located in Al-Ardiya area – Plot No. 66 – Block No. 3 – Plan M/36875, with an area of 600 m2, to be sold by public auction at a basic price of 405,000 K.D. (Four Hundred Five Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** According to the description certificate: - No violations. ** According to the expert's report: Expert session dated 1/7/2023: We moved to the disputed property located in Al-Ardiya area, Block 3, Street 9, House 21, Plot 66, Automated Number 12803986, in the presence and guidance of the plaintiff personally and her agent, and the first defendant personally and as an agent for the defendants from the second to the fifth, and in the presence of the expert of knowledge assisted by the Administration, Mr. Tariq Al-Moajel , to estimate the value of the property and the value of the usufruct for the property from 9/7/2011. The inspection was as follows: 1. The property subject of the lawsuit is located on one street and a back alley, bordered by a neighbor on two sides, and has a small setback in the front facade, paved and partially shaded, used as parking spaces. 2. The property subject of the lawsuit is a government house for private residence, with old finishes, its exterior cladding is limestone, and its area is (600) square meters according to the inheritance ownership document dated 6/11/2022, bearing number 11069, owned jointly by the plaintiff and the defendants from the first to the fifth, as stated in the aforementioned document. 3. The property subject of the lawsuit consists of a main building, two front annexes, and a back annex, according to the following details: - The main building consists of a ground floor and a first floor, according to the following details: * The ground floor consists of a living room, a reception hall, a bathroom, a kitchen, a maid's room, and its bathrooms. * The first floor consists of four rooms, a hall, and two bathrooms. - The first front annex consists of a room and a bathroom, and the other annex is a storage room. - The back annex consists of two floors, ground and first, according to the following details: The ground floor consists of a living room, a diwaniya , a room, three bathrooms, a maid's room, and a kitchen. The first floor is a room made of "Kirby" (prefabricated material). The disputed property has an old finishing level, and only the paints and flooring in the main building have been renovated according to the following details: the flooring is ceramic, the air conditioning is split units, the lighting is spotlights, the decorations are gypsum, and the walls have ordinary paint. It was found that the back annex is separate from the main building, with a separate entrance from the back alley, which the plaintiff enabled us to enter as she possesses the keys to the annex, and it is completely abandoned. The first defendant stated that the fifth defendant resides in the property and utilizes the main building and its attached front annex. It was found that the disputed property is not leased and generates no income. Mr. Expert of Knowledge assisted by the Administration inspected the property, recorded his observations, and reviewed the inheritance ownership document for the disputed property, in preparation for estimating the property's value and the value of its usufruct. Announcement of Property Sale by Public Auction The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the sale of the property described below by public auction on Monday, 19/5/2025 – Hall 48 – on the second floor of the Palace of Justice at 9:00 AM – in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2024/41 Sales/1 Filed by: Fahda Juma'an Al-Khailan Al-Enezi . Against: The heirs of the late Hamad Abdullah Al-Salal , namely: 1- Bader Hamad Abdullah Al-Salal . 2- Hala Hamad Abdullah Al-Salal . 3- Salwa Hamad Abdullah Al-Salal . Secondly: Auction Conditions: Firstly: The auction shall commence at the basic price specified for the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit, at the time of the sale session, the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees. Thirdly: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session to reduce the purchase price with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be re-opened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the initial bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid with the ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be re-opened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. No offer in this session will be considered valid unless accompanied by a deposit of its full value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the fees for transfer and registration of ownership, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to 200 K.D., legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in accordance with the law and is requested for bidders for the sale procedures, at their own responsibility, without the Clerk's Office at the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Notice: - 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . - 2- The auction judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . - 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the expropriated owner is residing in the property, they shall remain therein as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." President of the General Court Announcement of Sale of a Share in a Property by Public Auction The Clerk's Office at the General Court announces the public auction sale of a share in the property described below, on Monday, 2025/5/19 - 48 - on the second floor of the Palace of Justice at 9:00 AM - this is in execution of the court judgment issued in lawsuit No. 2024/84 Sales/1. Filed by: - Sabitan Ghazi Eqab Al-Mutairi Against: - 1- Mohammed Ali Hamad Al-Shammari . - 2- Hammad Ali Hamad Al-Shammari . - 3- Adel Miz'al Fahad Al-Shammari . - 4- Kuwait Credit Bank . Property Description: (According to the attached Description Certificate) - An undivided share of 50% in the property under Document No. 1992/5739, located in Al-Andalus area - Plot No. 17 - Block No. 5 - Plan M/33782, with a total area of 910m2, to be sold by public auction at a basic price of 306,000 K.D. (Three Hundred Six Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars) for the offered share. ** And according to what was stated in the Description Certificate: - Adhesion to the neighbor on the ground, first, and second floors towards the service street. - Adhesion in the middle of the plot. - Construction of light materials on the roof. ** And according to what was stated in the Expert's Report: - On 2024/4/16, the site of the dispute, located in Al-Andalus area - Block (5) - Street (1) - Plot (17) House (28), was visited in the presence and with the guidance of the plaintiff personally. The remaining parties to the lawsuit or their representatives failed to attend. In the presence of Mr. Fallah Nasser Murshid , the expert of knowledge at the administration, the inspection was carried out as follows: The property subject of the lawsuit conforms in terms of general specifications to the sale contract issued by the Real Estate Registration Department, registered under No. (1992/5739) and with an area of 910m2, submitted by the plaintiff's agent with their documents in the session on 2024/4/1, Document (1). The property subject of the lawsuit is a private residential plot located on two streets (Street (1) and Street (102)), bordered by a neighbor on two sides, and has (4) entrances on Street (1) side and (3) entrances on Street (102) side. The door and guard were knocked, and no one answered. Therefore, the expert of knowledge inspected the property subject of the lawsuit from the outside. The property subject of the lawsuit is a residential plot consisting of two parts, detailed as follows: The first part, with automated number (12770205), consists of a ground floor, first floor, and roof. The exterior cladding on Street (102) side is rough white limestone with pink accents, and the exterior cladding on Street (1) side is beige stone. The second part consists of a ground floor, first floor, and half of a second floor. The exterior cladding is rough beige limestone. The air conditioning of the property subject of the lawsuit is central and split units. The windows are aluminum, and there are shutters for the windows of the second part on Street (1) side. There are parking spaces on Street (102) side. The expert of knowledge inspected the property subject of the lawsuit in preparation for estimating the property's value (land and buildings and total value) and submitting the relevant report. He also reviewed the lawsuit file and its documents. upon him in the previous session, and no bid in this session shall be considered valid unless accompanied by a full deposit of its value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. The first apartment contains three rooms, two bathrooms, a kitchen, and a hall. The second apartment contains two rooms, two bathrooms, a kitchen, and a hall. - The rooftop contains an air conditioning area, a water heater room, and a small shed for storage. - The finishing quality of the property is medium, with ceramic floors, decorative ceilings, central air conditioning, and an elevator. Against: Khalid Khatlan Khamis Hadyan . Property Description: According to the attached description certificate. Property Document No. (2022/7122) located in Khaitan - Block No. (2), Plot No. (76) of Plan No. (M/30175), with an area of (300m2). *Note: There is a violation, which is an illegal construction on the first floor, consisting of a brick roof bridge. *Inspection The expert proceeded to inspect the property subject of the lawsuit, located in Khaitan - Block 10 - Street 8 - House No. 5 - Automated Number 12198712, with an area of 300m2, in the presence of the Department Expert Mr. Saad Al-Mutairi and the third plaintiff personally, Ahmed Khatlan Khamis Hadyan , as follows: * The expert found the following after careful examination and inspection, guided by those present, as the inspection was conducted from the outside in the presence of the third plaintiff personally: * The property is a private residence located on an internal street and bordered by neighbors from several sides. * The building is old, with 3 parking spaces and 2 floors. * The cladding type is yellow Sigma , and there are 2 entrances, with internal electrical and plumbing installations. * The flooring is porcelain type, the doors are iron, and the air conditioning type is units. * The resident of the property is the defendant Khalid Khamis Hadyan . * The Department Expert took the property specifications in preparation for estimating its market value and submitting the relevant report. * The expert decided to set a session for 5/1/2023 for the submission of the knowledge expert's report and to reserve the lawsuit for the report after being satisfied with what was presented before it. Second: Auction Conditions: First: The auction shall commence with a base price of 211,410 K.D. ("Two Hundred Eleven Thousand Four Hundred Ten Kuwaiti Dinars "). Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Execution Department at the Ministry of Justice . Third: If the person whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding shall be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price 2024/253 Sales/1 : Filed by: Jawaher Muhammad Jaze' Al-Ajmi . Against: 1- Mohammed Jazea Abdullah Al-Ajmi . 2- Raf'ah Mohammed Jazea Al-Ajmi . 3- Hammad Mohammed Jazea Al-Ajmi . Property Description (According to the attached Description Certificate) Property Document No. 1993/8946 located in Sabah Al-Salem area – Plot No. 120 – Block No. 13, Northern Part – Plan M/36873, with an area of 300.125 m2, to be sold by public auction at a base price of K.D. 342,000 (Three Hundred Forty-Two Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** According to the Description Certificate, the following violations were noted: - Full attachment from all sides on the ground floor, full attachment from the facade on the rear side (courtyard), attachment to a wall on the first floor, and attachment on the surrounding side. - And the presence of additional construction on the ground and first floors without a license. ** According to the Expert's Report: On 17/3/2021, the expert moved to the disputed property located in Sabah Al-Salem area – Block 13 – First Street, Plot 41, and inspected it on site in the presence and guidance of the appellant personally, the first respondent personally, and the agent of the second and third respondents, with the assistance of Mr. Study Expert at the Department / Tariq Abdullah Al-Moajel . The inspection was carried out as follows: 1- The disputed property is a private residence with front and back access, and a side opening to an electrical transformer. It is bordered by neighbors on one side and has side and rear setbacks. It is a government-issued property with an area of 300.125 m2, renovated from an old statement, and fully clad with white stone from the outside. 2- The building has a relatively modern statement, with an ivory and gray Sigma facade, and has parking spaces in front of it, and two entrances from the inside. 3- The expert knocked on the door, and one of the residents opened it and stated that the first defendant does not reside in the property and that it is leased to others. 4- The expert inspected the property from the outside and found numbered doors on the apartments. 5- It became clear to the expert from the site that there are 2 basements, a ground floor, first and second floors, and a roof, which is in violation of the description certificate issued by Kuwait Municipality . It also became clear that one of the basements is residential and the other is for car parking. 6- The disputed property consists of a ground floor and a first floor, with nothing on the roof, with the following details: a- The ground floor consists of 4 rooms + 4 bathrooms + a hall + a kitchen + a storage room. b- The first floor consists of 5 rooms + 3 bathrooms + a large hall + a large kitchen. 7- The exterior cladding is white stone. 8- The house's air conditioning consists of separate units. 9- Ceramic floors and no elevator. 10- The Study Expert took the necessary observations from the outside in preparation for estimating the value of the property (land and building) and the value of the quarter from the outside. 11- The expert took photographs of the disputed property. Secondly: Auction Terms: Firstly: The auction shall start at the base price indicated for the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: Whoever's bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Thirdly: If the bidder whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price, otherwise the bidding will be re-opened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the next session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be re-opened in the same session based on this new price. Fifthly: If the first bidder does not deposit the full price in the next session and no one comes forward with a ten percent increase, the bidding will be immediately re-opened at their expense based on the price at which the sale was awarded in the previous session, and no bid not accompanied by the deposit of its full value will be considered in this session. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and registration fees for ownership, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to K.D. 200, legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement is published in application of the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures are responsible for them, without the Clerk's Office at the Court of First Instance bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property, and this inspection negates any claim of ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obliged to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent." There is also a driver's room with an isolated bathroom, accessible via the side entrance. * The ground floor consists of (1) hall, (1) master bedroom with a bathroom, a main kitchen, a dining room overlooking the hall, and (1) bathroom. * The first floor consists of a hall, a kitchen, (5) rooms, and (4) bathrooms, including (2) master bedrooms. * The second floor consists of (2) apartments, each containing (1) hall, (3) rooms, (2) bathrooms including (1) master bedroom, (1) kitchen, and a maid's room with a bathroom. The expert found that both apartments are occupied, as the resident of Apartment No. (1), Mr. Bader Al-Anazi , stated that he has not paid rent since 2016 until the present date, but before 2016, he used to pay rent of (400 K.D.). Mr. Abdul Hassan Al-Ajmi , resident of Apartment No. (2), stated that he pays a monthly rent of (300 K.D.) until the present date. * The roof consists of a quarter floor, comprising a laundry room, a master bedroom with a bathroom, and a storage room. There is also a "large" external tank and a central water heater room. * The subject property has (2) elevators; the first elevator reaches from the basement to the roof, and the second elevator reaches the floors from the ground floor to the second floor. * There are (2) staircases; the first staircase reaches from the basement to the roof, and the second staircase reaches from the basement to the first floor. * The expert found that all floors are connected, except for the second floor, which contains two apartments. * The property is new, with ceramic flooring, wallpaper decorations in various places, white gypsum ceilings, spot lighting, and hidden lighting. * The doors are made of wood, and the air conditioning type in the subject property is central air conditioning. * The defendant stated that he occupies only the master bedroom on the ground floor, and the first defendant stated that she occupies only her room on the first floor. * Mr. Expert of Knowledge took all his observations in preparation for drafting his report. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees immediately upon the sale session. Third: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price, otherwise the bidding will be reopened at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was previously awarded. Fourth: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to them, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full auction price. In this case, the bidding will be reopened in the same session based on this new price. Fifth: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid with the ten percent increase, the bidding will immediately be reopened at their expense based on the price at which it was awarded to them in the previous session. No offer in this session will be considered unless accompanied by a full deposit of its value. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixth: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the transfer and registration fees for ownership, enforcement procedure expenses amounting to K.D. 200, legal and expert fees, and advertising and publication costs for the sale in daily newspapers. Seventh: This announcement is published in accordance with the law, and claimants for sale procedures are responsible for their actions without any liability on the part of the Court Clerk's Office . Eighth: The successful bidder acknowledges that they have inspected the property in a manner that negates any ignorance of its condition. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of its pronouncement, in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedure . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedure states that "If the dispossessed owner is residing in the property, they shall remain in it as a tenant by operation of law, and the successful bidder shall be obliged to draw up a lease contract in their favor at a fair market rent."
