Property Document No. 2011/3718, located in Al-Jabriya area - Plot (451), Block No. (5), Plan 31742 A - with an area of 580m², to be sold by public auction at a base price of 530,000 K.D. (Five Hundred Thirty Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ). ** It is stated in the Property Description Certificate that the property is located within a private residential area and consists of a basement, ground floor, and first floor. ** And according to the expert's report: The disputed property, located in Al-Jabriya area, Block (5), Street (16), House (12), with automated number 15176121, was inspected as follows: - The disputed property is a private residence located on an internal street with a side yard used as a garden and includes parking spaces. The property area is 580m², which matches document No. 2011/3718. The house consists of a basement, ground floor, first floor, and a quarter floor on the roof. It has 4 entrances, central air conditioning, no elevator, and pink paint finishing. It has been divided into two sections: one independent section by the plaintiff and another independent section by the defendant. The parties guided the expert regarding the property's components as per the plan submitted for expertise, which are as follows: 1- Section belonging to the Plaintiff * The basement consists of a sunken courtyard + (2) halls + (4) rooms + kitchen + (2) bathrooms + washbasins. * Ground floor: hall + room + washbasins + bathroom. The representative of the plaintiff stated that the basement is not utilized, and the expert noted that the keys were in her possession and that of her representative (agent), so the door was not opened. 2- Section belonging to the Defendant: * Ground floor: hall + kitchen + dining room + (2) rooms + bathroom. * First floor: consists of two apartments with a shared kitchen between them. * The first apartment is independent and occupied by the parties' daughter, consisting of a hall + room + bathroom. * The second apartment is independent and occupied by the parties' son, consisting of a hall + (4) rooms + (2) bathrooms. * Annexes: driver's room + storage room + bathroom. * Roof: open and shared, with (2) rooms that the expert could not inspect, and the parties stated that there were storage rooms. The expert, along with Mr. Expert Diraya , inspected the property on-site and collected relevant data in preparation for drafting a report on its market value.

Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . Secondly: The bidder whose offer is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Thirdly: If the bidder whose offer was approved fails to deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding shall be re-conducted at their expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction shall be awarded to them, unless another bidder comes forward The property is a private residence consisting of a ground floor, first floor, and a roof, according to the Municipality's letter. Second: Auction Conditions: First: The auction shall commence at a base price of K.D. 422,820 (Four Hundred Twenty-Two Thousand Eight Hundred Twenty Kuwaiti Dinars). Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Enforcement Department at the Ministry of Justice . On-site, in the presence and under the guidance of the plaintiff's agent and the first defendant's agent, and with the assistance of Mr. Al-Darariya Expert in the Department / Saad Mubarak Al-Mutairi , the inspection was carried out as follows: 1- The disputed property is located on a single internal street, bordered by a neighbor on the right and a neighbor on the left, and directly overlooks the sea. 2- It contains only a ground floor and an external annex. 3- The exterior cladding is white mosaic. 4- The ground floor contains four master bedrooms with their bathrooms, a maid's room with its bathroom, two living rooms, in addition to a third hall with a dining table and two preparatory kitchens. 5- The finishing of the disputed property consists of marble floors, gypsum board decorative ceilings, and plain smooth painted walls, with one wall in the main hall finished with wallpaper. 6- The external annex consists of two rooms for the guard with a bathroom. 7- There is a balcony directly overlooking the sea. 8- There are parking spaces for cars inside the plot. The first defendant stated that the keys to the disputed property are in the possession of the plaintiff. First: Descriptions of the first defendant's property according to the attached description certificate. 3- Property Document No. 2012/13569 located in Sabah Al-Ahmad Sea City - Plot No. 499 - from Plan No. M / 2 A37500, with an area of 1437 m². This will be by public auction with a basic price of /470,000 K.D. (Four Hundred Seventy Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) (vacant land). *Inspection: On 27/9/2022, the disputed property was visited, which is a vacant land located in Sabah Al-Ahmad Sea City - Block A2 - Plot 499 - Automated Address Number 95151206, Document No. (13569) dated 6/11/2012, with an area of (1437) square meters. It was inspected on-site in the presence and under the guidance of the first defendant's agent and with the assistance of Mr. Al-Darariya Expert in the Department / Saad Mubarak Al-Mutairi , and the inspection was carried out as follows: 1- The disputed property is a vacant land located on a single internal street and directly overlooks the sea. 2- The disputed property is bordered by a neighbor on the right and a neighbor on the left. 3- The disputed property contains an iron mesh fence, five trees, a pergola seating area, and children's play equipment. 4- The sea oasis is 21 meters long. The attending parties to the lawsuit stated that what is on the land does not belong to any of the parties to the lawsuit. Second: Auction Conditions: First: The auction shall start with the basic price associated with the property. Participation in the auction requires payment of at least one-fifth of that price by a certified check from the drawee bank or by a letter of guarantee from one of the banks in favor of the Execution Department at the Ministry of Justice . Second: Whoever's bid is approved by the judge must deposit the full approved price, expenses, and registration fees at the time of the sale session. Third: If the bidder whose bid was approved does not deposit the full price, they must deposit at least one-fifth of the price, otherwise it shall be re-opened.

chunks.title: Disputed Property Located in Al-Masail <orig>المسائل</orig> Area, Block 5 – Street 511 – Plot 132
chunks.text_en: The inspection was conducted as follows: 1- The disputed property is a private residence plot of medium construction, located on a single street with a western facade, bordered by neighbors on 3 sides. Its exterior cladding is made of stone and sigma, and it consists of a basement, a ground floor, a first floor, a half second floor, and a roof. It has 2 entrances: a main entrance leading to the plot and a side entrance leading to the basement. 2- The area of the disputed property is 400 square meters, which matches ownership document No. 4759/2017 dated 29/6/2017, provided by the plaintiff's agent during the session on 29/8/2022. 3- Those present stated that they could not enable the expert to inspect the disputed property from the inside. 4- The plaintiff's agent submitted plans for the property showing its internal components. Both parties acknowledged the accuracy of the plans and the components mentioned therein, and the plans were approved by both parties to the lawsuit. 5- After reviewing the plans, the expert stated that he could estimate the property's value based on the external inspection and the submitted plans. 6- According to the submitted plans, the components of the disputed property are as follows: - The basement consists of a main kitchen, (1) room, a bathroom, a courtyard, a laundry room, a maid's room with a bathroom – a driver's room with a bathroom. - The ground floor: consists of (3) rooms, a kitchen, a reception hall, (1) bathroom and washbasins. <header>

chunks.text_en: Secondly: If the bidder whose offer was approved does not deposit the full price, he must deposit at least one-fifth of the price; otherwise, the bidding will be re-opened at his expense in the same session based on the price at which the sale was awarded. Fourthly: If the bidder deposits the price in the subsequent session, the auction will be awarded to him, unless someone comes forward in that session willing to purchase with a ten percent increase, accompanied by the deposit of the full price. In this case, the bidding will be re-opened in the same session based on the price. Fifthly: If the first bidder fails to deposit the full price in the subsequent session and no one comes forward to bid with a ten percent increase, the bidding will be immediately re-opened at his expense based on the price at which it was awarded to him in the previous session, and no offer in this session not accompanied by a deposit of its full value will be considered. The defaulting bidder shall be liable for any shortfall in the property's price. Sixthly: In all cases, the successful bidder shall bear the fees for transfer and registration of ownership, the expenses of execution procedures amounting to 200 KWD, legal and expert fees, and the costs of advertising and publishing the sale in daily newspapers. Seventhly: This announcement shall be published in accordance with the law, and those directly involved in the sale procedures shall be responsible for them, without the Registry Department of the General Court bearing any responsibility. Eighthly: The successful bidder acknowledges that he has inspected the property with an inspection that negates ignorance. Notice: 1- This sale announcement shall be published in the Official Gazette in accordance with Article 266 of the Code of Civil Procedures . 2- The auction award judgment is subject to appeal within seven days from the date of pronouncement of the judgment in accordance with Article 277 of the Code of Civil Procedures . 3- The last paragraph of Article 276 of the Code of Civil Procedures states that "If the person whose property has been expropriated is residing in the property, he shall remain therein as a tenant by force of law, and the successful bidder shall be obligated to draw up a lease contract in his favor at a fair market rent." chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: Practice No. (MEW / 6 / 2021-2022) Civil Works for the Establishment, Completion, and Maintenance of the Electricity Emergency Center in Khaitan Area. The Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy announces the offering of the aforementioned practice. Practice documents can be obtained from the Contracts and Tenders Department (Tenders Control) in the main building of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy (South Surra ) - Sixth Ring Road, First Floor, according to the following data: - Offering Date: 4 / 5 / 2025. - Closing Date: 2 / 6 / 2025. - Document Price: (50) K.D. (Fifty Kuwaiti Dinars only). - Initial Security Deposit: (10,000) K.D. (Ten Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars only) valid for (90) days in favor of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy . - This practice: General - This practice: Non-divisible.

- Alternative Offers: Submission of alternative offers is not permitted. - Final Time for Submission of Bids: At 1:00 PM on the day specified in the above announcement for depositing bids, and no bid will be accepted thereafter. - The aforementioned cash payment shall be made by K-Net card. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises are exempted from these fees in accordance with the text of Article (39) of Law (74) of 2019 regarding amendments to Public Tenders Law No. (49) of 2016. - Owners of small and medium-sized enterprises wishing to apply for the practice must attach with their bid a valid certificate issued by the National Fund for Small and Medium Enterprise Care and Development , including their accreditation as a small or medium enterprise. - A certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage issued by the Ministry of Social Affairs and Labor , valid for one year, must be submitted with the bid. - Bids must be submitted accompanied by a certificate from the Kuwait Chamber of Commerce and Industry stating the company's registration for the year of the offering. - Documents shall be placed in sealed envelopes, stamped with red wax, clearly marked with the practice number and subject, and deposited in the bids box of the Contracts and Tenders Department (Tenders Control), First Floor, in the main building of the Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy , South Surra - Sixth Ring Road. - A preliminary meeting for participants in the practice will be held (10) working days from the date of the practice offering, i.e., at 10:00 AM on 25 / 5 / 2025, at the office of the Director of Electrical Distribution Networks Department . - For inquiries: Please contact Engineer Marwan Al-Qattan / 99922652 at the Contracts and Tenders Department, Fax No.: 25371531. Therefore, all relevant state ministries, all banks, companies, and other individuals who have a right or a debt to the aforementioned persons are requested to review the Estates Department at the Administration during official working hours, within (15) days from the date of publication of this announcement in the Official Gazette . After this period, the Administration will not be responsible for any disposition of the estate to its beneficiaries. Head of the General Department of Enforcement

chunks.title: Announcement
chunks.text_en: The Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy announces the offering of the practices mentioned below. Practice documents can be obtained electronically from the website https://eprocurement.mew.gov.kw This is within thirty days from the date of this letter. We refer to the provision of Article 8 of Income Tax Decree No. 3 of 1955, as amended by Law No. 2 of 2008, which stipulates a penalty of 1% for every thirty days or part thereof in case of delay in payment, and Article (24) of the Executive Regulations regarding objection to tax assessment , which stipulates that a tax objection submitted by the company shall not be considered after sixty days from the date of notification of the assessment letter. Undersecretary of the Ministry of Finance

Announcement The Public Authority for Industry announces that Messrs. / Heirs of Abdulaziz Khalifa Farhan Al-Khalidi have applied for the issuance of a replacement for the lost parcel contract No. (26), Plot 1, in South Amghara area. Anyone in possession of the aforementioned parcel contract or having any information regarding it is requested to proceed to the headquarters of the Public Authority for Industry (Ground Floor), Licenses and Industrial Registry Department, within two weeks from the date of publication of this announcement, and accordingly, the Authority will, after the expiry of the deadline, issue a replacement for the lost contract. Public Authority for Civil Information Announcement of Tender No. PACI-1-2025/2026 Regarding the investment of a food sales and services site at the Authority's main headquarters The Public Authority for Civil Information announces the publication of a simple tender in a sealed envelope regarding the investment of a food sales and services site at the Authority's main headquarters, in accordance with the terms and specifications contained in the booklet: 1- These documents can be obtained from the Financial Affairs Department - Cashier Section (South Surra - Ministries Area - Ground Floor) during official working hours for a fee of K.D. 50/- (Fifty Kuwaiti Dinars only). 2- The deadline for submitting bids to the Financial Affairs Department is 12:00 PM on Thursday, 8/5/2025. Any bid submitted after this deadline will be rejected. 3- The preliminary meeting will be held at 12:00 PM on Sunday, 4/5/2025, for companies wishing to participate in the tender at the Authority's main headquarters in South Surra / Multi-purpose Hall on the Ground Floor. 4- The amount of the preliminary guarantee must not be less than 2% (two percent) of the tender bid value and must be a certified check or a guarantee issued by one of the approved local banks in Arabic and valid throughout the bid validity period. 5- The final guarantee amount is 10% (ten percent) of the total value, provided that this guarantee is issued by one of the approved local banks in Arabic. 6- Direct execution of the contract from the date of contracting. 7- The bid remains valid for 90 days (ninety days) from the date of opening the bid envelopes. 8- Alternative offers are not accepted. 9- The auction is indivisible. Acting Director General Announcement The Public Authority for Civil Information announces that the individuals whose names are listed below have had their residential addresses removed from its records (based on the declaration of the property owner or due to building demolition). These individuals must visit the Authority to register a new address (after providing supporting documents) within thirty days from this date, otherwise they will be subject to the penalty stipulated in Article (33) of Law No. 32 of 1982, a fine not exceeding one hundred dinars, multiplied by the number of individuals. the auction site on the specified date, observing the following terms of sale: 1. The Auction Sale Committee may sell the goods in one lot or several lots, depending on the sale conditions. 2. The successful bidder shall bear a brokerage commission of 2% of the sale price. 3. The successful bidder must pay the value of the goods only via K-net , as determined by the Auction Sale Committee . 4. The successful bidder must remove the goods from the Bait Al-Mal Control Department premises urgently and within 5 (five) to 7 (seven) days after the auction is awarded to them. Otherwise, any goods will be seized and sold in the next auction if the successful bidder fails to remove them within the specified period. 5. If the goods are not sold for any reason in the auction, the committee has the right to offer them for sale in the subsequent auction. 6. The administration has the right to stop, postpone, or re-schedule the sale at another time if a suitable price is not obtained or for any reasons it deems contrary to its interest. 7. The Auction Sale Committee may cancel or postpone the auction for any reason it deems appropriate. 8. Since the goods will be sold as-is, bidders must inspect them to ascertain their condition, quality, and type at the address mentioned above during the auction sale. 9. In the event that the successful bidder refrains from paying the sale price or part thereof, the General Administration of Customs has the right to re-sell on their account. If the price does not reach the amount for which the auction was initially awarded, they are obligated to pay the difference. In this case, the administration has the right to bar them from participating in any future auctions. 10. The administration is not responsible for any action or use of the sold goods that violates the prevailing laws and legislations. 11. This sale is conducted in accordance with the provisions of Law No. 10 of 2003 regarding the Unified Customs System and its regulating decisions. 12. All bidders must adhere to health and preventive requirements. Auction Sale Committee List of Individuals and Their Civil ID Numbers

chunks.title: Tender No. (8-2023/2022) <orig>المزايدة رقم (8-2023/2022)</orig>
chunks.text_en:
The Directorate General of Civil Aviation announces the extension of the closing period for Tender No. (8-2023/2022) for the supply, installation, operation, maintenance, management, and training of the Passenger Registration and Early Border Security Inquiry System at Kuwait International Airport . Please note that the final deadline for submitting bids will be at 1:00 PM on Monday, 30/6/2025, instead of Wednesday, 30/4/2025. Any bid submitted after this deadline will be rejected. chunks.title: Announcement No. 46/2025
chunks.text_en:
The holder of the following license has submitted a request to the Municipality: License Holder Information | Commercial Name | Al-Dahi International General Trading and Construction Contracting Company | |:---|:---| | Category | Municipality Contractor, Category D | | Registration No. | 7200268 |
requesting the release of the company's bank guarantees deposited with the Municipality in consideration of its classification. Based on Article (19) of Municipality Decision No. (161/2009) regarding the classification, licensing, and registration of building and demolition contractors in Kuwait Municipality , which stipulates that a contractor may submit a request to the Municipality to cancel their registration before its expiry or to cancel the classification, and the committee stipulated in Article One of this regulation may issue its decision thereon after fulfilling the following two conditions: 1- The contractor has completed all contracted works. 2- Fifteen days have passed from the date of publication of this request in the Official Gazette . The cancellation of the license or registration entails the return of the bank guarantee submitted by the contractor, taking into account the last paragraph of Article (10) of this decision. Accordingly, the Municipality appeals to anyone who has any claims against the aforementioned contractor regarding construction contracts concluded between them that have not yet been completed, or judicial attachments in their favor due to violating contracting works or dues arising from the contracting agreement, to review the Contractors Section in the Engineering Systems Department within a maximum period of fifteen days from this date. The Municipality will not consider any claims submitted after the prescribed period. Director General of the Municipality

1- Tender documents can be obtained starting from Sunday, 27/04/2025, and on regular working days during official working hours from the Procurement Department's website (https://pur.ku.edu.kw). 2- The deadline for submitting bids is Tuesday, 20/05/2025, at 12:00 PM. Bids received after this deadline will not be considered, and bids will be opened in a public session. 3- Instructions for Bidders: - Bids must be submitted via optical discs (CDs) (3 copies), ensuring that the copying process for all files has been completed correctly before depositing the discs in the submission envelope. The discs must be identical, similar, protected, non-modifiable, and each must contain all tender documents signed and stamped with the bidder's (company's) seal. - Inquiries are accepted within 10 days from the date of the announcement. No inquiries will be accepted after the specified deadline. - The following paper documents must be submitted: - The original copy of the bid form and undertaking. - Initial security deposit (as per the tender announcement). - Purchase receipt from the website. - A copy of a valid national labor percentage certificate. - A valid 'To Whom It May Concern' certificate issued by the Public Authority for Manpower , stating the absence of the envelope designated for the tender. - The bid will be disqualified if any document mentioned in the instructions is not submitted. - Bids are to be placed in the designated box at the Procurement Department – at Sabah Al-Salem University City (Al-Shaddadiya ) – University Administration Building – Sixth Floor – Suppliers Affairs Section . Note: Tender documents are to be collected by companies specialized in this field, provided they are registered with the University's Suppliers Affairs Section and registered with the Central Agency for Public Tenders during official working hours.
Secretary General of the University

The bid will be excluded if the required documents are not attached. - Bids are to be placed in the designated bid box at the Procurement Department in Sabah Al-Salem University City (Al-Shaddadiya) – University Administration Building – 6th Floor – Suppliers Affairs Section . To purchase the practice documents and obtain the compact disc (CD) related to the practice, please visit the main headquarters of the Public Institution for Social Insurance located in Al-Mirqab area – Al-Sour Street – or call phone numbers (22994472 – 55538757). (Two Thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ) valid for (90) days |
Preliminary Meeting (Inquiry Session): None 1. The aforementioned cash payment must be made only via "K-Net" at the Institution's treasury. 2. Bids are to be submitted at the main headquarters of the Public Institution for Social Insurance – Al-Mirqab – in the bid box located on the ground floor. The final deadline for submitting bids is 1:00 PM. 3. For any reservations or inquiries regarding the aforementioned practice, please send them to the following email address from 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM, no later than Thursday, 2025-5-15. Requests submitted after this time will not be considered: Procurement@pifss.gov.kw 4. The right to attend the preliminary meeting – if any – is restricted to companies participating in the practice, provided they bring an official letter from the practitioner authorizing the representative to attend the meeting, a copy of their civil ID, and a copy of the practice document purchase receipt. The following documents must be submitted when purchasing the practice documents: 1. An authorization letter from the company containing the name and civil ID number of the authorized person for purchasing the practice documents. 2. A copy of the civil ID of the authorized person for purchasing the practice documents. Additionally, copies of the following documents must be attached inside the bid envelope: 1. A recent copy of the company's license from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . 2. A recent copy of the registration certificate from the Central Agency for Public Tenders . 3. A recent copy of the registration certificate from the Central Agency for Information Technology . 4. A recent copy of the certificate of compliance with the national labor percentage issued by the Public Authority for Manpower . 5. A recent clearance certificate stating no suspension codes on the practitioner's file with the Public Authority for Manpower . Companies participating in the tender must refer to the Public Authority for Housing Welfare – South Surra – Third Floor – Documents and Contracts Department , bringing with them a request for refund of the tender purchase value, attached with the original receipt of the tender documents purchase, and an authorization from the company to submit the request. The Director General

chunks.text_en: Announcement Kuwait Credit Bank announces the floating of Tender No. (3-2025/2026) for the maintenance of the Digital Transformation system and devices. Interested parties wishing to submit their bids must refer to the Bank to purchase the booklet of terms and technical specifications for the aforementioned practice, according to the details specified below:

chunks.title: Conditions for the Submitted Bid
chunks.text_en: * The bidder must submit a technical offer and a financial offer in two sealed envelopes, one for the technical offer and the other for the financial offer, taking into consideration attaching a copy of the bid on a flash drive, according to the following conditions: * The technical envelope must contain the following: * Initial security in the form of a letter of guarantee for the amount of (K.D. 1000) "only one thousand Kuwaiti Dinars ", unconditional, free from any reservations, and irrevocable, issued by one of the banks approved by the State of Kuwait in the name and for the benefit of Kuwait Credit Bank , provided that this security is valid for at least three months from the last date for receiving bids. Any bid not accompanied by the full amount of this security will be excluded. Initial securities may not be refunded until after (90) ninety days from the closing date of the practice or when the winning bidder provides the final security and signs the contract, unless the practice is cancelled. Kuwait Credit Bank will not pay any interest on the amount of this security, and the bidder's creditors may not seize its amount. * General terms, specifications, and technical data for the submitted offer. * Complete data on Kuwaiti subcontracting companies or individuals who may be assigned part of the execution, if the practice documents require it. * Any other documents or data that the practice documents may require. * The financial envelope must contain the following: * Bid format approved by the bidder. * Price lists and quantity schedules. * Any other elements that may affect the financial value of the offer according to the terms of the tender. * Any other documents or data required by the practice documents. 6. Certificate of payment of contributions to the Public Institution for Social Security . 7. Copy of the company registration form in the Institution's suppliers and contractors register. The Director General for Costs

chunks.title: Public Authority for Housing Welfare <orig>المؤسسة العامة للرعاية السكنية</orig>
chunks.text_en: Announcement of Company Registration The Public Authority for Housing Welfare announces the commencement of receiving applications from companies wishing to register as approved suppliers with the Authority, in addition to updating the data of companies already registered in its suppliers' register for the fiscal year 2025/2026. Please note that all purchasing operations will be conducted through the Authority's external website.

chunks.title: Required Activities:
chunks.text_en: * Stationery and office supplies – Computer hardware and accessories – Office equipment – Electronic and technical devices – Publications – Advertising and publicity – Hospitality and gift presentation – Furniture and furnishings – Agriculture – Translation offices – Auditing offices – Firefighting equipment – Cooling spare parts – Blacksmithing and carpentry works – Transportation services – Car rental – Hotels – Insurance services – Food supplies and hotel necessities – Training services – Exhibitions (communication and networks).

chunks.title: Required Documents for Registration or Update:
chunks.text_en: * Commercial license from the Ministry of Commerce and Industry . * Certificate of incorporation and license from the Ministry of Justice . * Commercial name and number. * Certificate from the Public Authority for Civil Information (Civil ID Number). * Phone numbers. * Address. * Nature of activity. * Bank account number in IBAN format. * Contact details (email).

chunks.title: Method of Application:
chunks.text_en: * Payment of due fees for registration and update at the Revenue and Collection Department – Revenue and Cashier Section for an amount of K.D. 20.000 (twenty Kuwaiti Dinars ). * Applications are submitted at the Authority's main building in the Ministries Area – First Floor – Procurement Department from 01/05/2025 until 31/12/2025, from 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM. * For review and inquiries, please contact phone number 25301072. * Or by correspondence via email purchases@pahw.gov.kw. <footer> Announcement Tender Cancellation No.: (PAHW/M/ 1379 – 2024 / 2025) The Public Authority for Housing Welfare announces the cancellation of Tender No.: (PAHW/M/ 1379 – 2024 / 2025) concerning the provision of a work team to provide technical support to the Authority's sectors. </footer>
